       \AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Probability"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_bw}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_fill\_grey}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{start =} \FloatTok{0.8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{end =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \CommentTok{\# Gives different shades}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{family =} \StringTok{"sans"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{family =} \StringTok{"sans"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"bottom"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{legend.justification =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{, }
    \AttributeTok{axis.text.x =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{vjust =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{plot.subtitle =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"italic"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.background =} \FunctionTok{element\_rect}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"grey90"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{strip.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }

\part{Latent Profile Analysis}\label{part-latent-profile-analysis}

\chapter{Enumeration}\label{lpa-enum}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\emph{Example: PISA Student Data}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  The first example closely follows the vignette used to demonstrate the \href{https://data-edu.github.io/tidyLPA/articles/Introduction_to_tidyLPA.html}{tidyLPA} package (Rosenberg, 2019).
\end{enumerate}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  This model utilizes the \texttt{PISA} data collected in the U.S. in 2015. To learn more about this data \href{http://www.oecd.org/pisa/data/}{see here}.
\item
  To access the 2015 US \texttt{PISA} data \& documentation in R use the following code:
\end{itemize}

Variables:

\begin{description}
\item[\texttt{broad\_interest}]
composite measure of students' self reported broad interest
\item[\texttt{enjoyment}]
composite measure of students' self reported enjoyment
\item[\texttt{instrumental\_mot}]
composite measure of students' self reported instrumental motivation
\item[\texttt{self\_efficacy}]
composite measure of students' self reported self efficacy
\end{description}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\#devtools::install\_github("jrosen48/pisaUSA15")}
\CommentTok{\#library(pisaUSA15)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Latent Profile Models}\label{latent-profile-models}

Latent Profile Analysis (LPA) is a statistical modeling approach for estimating distinct profiles of variables and uses continuous - rather than
categorical - indicators. Unlike latent class analysis, which uses categorical indicators, LPA models the means, variances, and covariances of continuous indicators within each latent profile.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Terminology for specifying variance-covariance matrix}\label{terminology-for-specifying-variance-covariance-matrix}

The code used to estimate LPA models in this walkthrough is from the \texttt{tidyLPA} package.
\texttt{TidyLPA}(\href{https://data-edu.github.io/tidyLPA/articles/Introduction_to_tidyLPA.html}{source}) is an R package designed to estimate latent profile models using a tidy framework.
It can interface with Mplus via the MplusAutomation package, enabling the estimation of latent profile models with different variance-covariance structures.

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  \texttt{model\ 1} Profile-invariant / Diagonal: Equal variances, and covariances fixed to 0
\item
  \texttt{model\ 2} Profile-varying / Diagonal: Free variances and covariances fixed to 0
\item
  \texttt{model\ 3} Profile-invariant / Non-Diagonal: Equal variances and equal covariances

  \begin{itemize}
  \tightlist
  \item
    Note: an alternative to Model 3 is freely estimating the covariances
  \end{itemize}
\item
  \texttt{model\ 4} Free variances, and equal covariances
\item
  \texttt{model\ 5} Equal variances, and free covariances
\item
  \texttt{model\ 6} Profile Varying / Non-Diagonal: Free variances and free covariances
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Model 1}\label{model-1}

\emph{Profile-invariant/diagonal:}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Equal Variances}: Variances are fixed to equality across the profiles (i.e., variances are constrained to be equal for each profile).
\item
  \emph{Covariances fixed to zero} (i.e., the off-diagonal cells of the matrix are zero).
\end{itemize}

The most parsimonious model and the most restricted.

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_1 & 0 & 0 \\
0 & \sigma^2_2 & 0 \\
0 & 0 & \sigma^2_3 \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\subsection{Model 2}\label{model-2}

\emph{Profile-varying/diagonal:}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Free variances}: Variances parameters are freely estimated across the profiles (i.e., variances vary by profile).
\item
  \emph{Covariances} \emph{fixed to zero} (i.e., the off-diagonal cells of the matrix are zero).
\end{itemize}

This model is more flexible and less parsimonious than model 1.

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_{1p} & 0 & 0 \\
0 & \sigma^2_{2p} & 0 \\
0 & 0 & \sigma^2_{3p} \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\subsection{Model 3}\label{model-3}

\emph{Profile-invariant/ non-diagonal or unrestricted:}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Equal variances:} Variances are fixed to equality across profile.
  (i.e., variances are constrained to be same for each profile).
\item
  \emph{Equal Covariances}: The covariances are now estimated and constrained to be equal.

  \begin{itemize}
  \tightlist
  \item
    An alternative to Model 3 is freely estimating the covariances (Model 5 here).
  \end{itemize}
\end{itemize}

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_1 & \sigma_{12} & \sigma_{13} \\
\sigma_{12} & \sigma^2_2 & \sigma_{23} \\
\sigma_{13} & \sigma_{23} & \sigma^2_3 \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\subsection{Model 4}\label{model-4}

Varying means, varying variances, and equal covariances:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Free variances}: Variances parameters are freely estimated across profiles (i.e., variances vary by profile).
\item
  \emph{Equal Covariances}: Covariances are constrained to be equal.
\end{itemize}

This model is also considered to be an extension of Model 3.

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_{1p} & \sigma_{12} & \sigma_{13} \\
\sigma_{12} & \sigma^2_{2p} & \sigma_{23} \\
\sigma_{13} & \sigma_{23} & \sigma^2_{3p} \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\subsection{Model 5}\label{model-5}

Varying means, equal variances, and varying covariances:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Equal variances:} Variances are fixed to equality across the profiles.
  (i.e., variances are constrained to be same for each profile).
\item
  \emph{Free Covariances}: Covariances are now freely estimated across the profiles.
\end{itemize}

This model is also considered to be an extension of Model 3.

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_{1} & \sigma_{12p} & \sigma_{13p} \\
\sigma_{12p} & \sigma^2_{2} & \sigma_{23p} \\
\sigma_{13p} & \sigma_{23p} & \sigma^2_{3} \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\subsection{Model 6}\label{model-6}

\emph{Profile-varying / Non-diagonal}:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{Free variances}: Variances parameters are freely estimated across profiles (i.e., variances vary by profile).
\item
  \emph{Free Covariances}: Covariances are now freely estimated across the profiles.
\end{itemize}

This is the most complex and unrestricted model.
It is also the least parsimonious

\emph{Note}: The unrestricted model is also sometimes known as Model 4.

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma^2_{1p} & \sigma_{12p} & \sigma_{13p} \\
\sigma_{12p} & \sigma^2_{2p} & \sigma_{23p} \\
\sigma_{13p} & \sigma_{23p} & \sigma^2_{3p} \\
\end{pmatrix}
\]

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Prepare Data}\label{prepare-data-4}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{pisa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pisaUSA15[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,] }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(broad\_interest, enjoyment, instrumental\_mot, self\_efficacy)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Enumeration}\label{enumeration-3}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{\texttt{tidyLPA}}{tidyLPA}}\label{tidylpa}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Enumerate using \texttt{estimate\_profiles()}:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  Estimate models with profiles \(K = 1:5\)
\item
  Model has 4 continuous indicators
\item
  Default variance-covariance specifications (model 1)
\item
  Change \texttt{variances} and \texttt{covariances} to indicate the model you want to specify, in this example, we are estimating all six models.
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\CommentTok{\# Run LPA models }
\NormalTok{lpa\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pisa }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{estimate\_profiles}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{,}
                      \AttributeTok{package =} \StringTok{"MplusAutomation"}\NormalTok{,}
                      \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} \StringTok{"starts = 500 100;"}\NormalTok{,}
                      \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat residual tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
                      \AttributeTok{variances =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"equal"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"varying"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"equal"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"varying"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"equal"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"varying"}\NormalTok{),}
                      \AttributeTok{covariances =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"zero"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"zero"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"equal"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"equal"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"varying"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"varying"}\NormalTok{),}
                      \AttributeTok{keepfiles =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Compare fit statistics}
\FunctionTok{get\_fit}\NormalTok{(lpa\_fit)}


\CommentTok{\# Move files to folder }
\NormalTok{files }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"\^{}model"}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{move\_files}\NormalTok{(files, }\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{overwrite =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{\texorpdfstring{\texttt{Mplus}}{Mplus}}\label{mplus}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Alternative method to \texttt{estimate\_profiles()}: Run enumeration using \texttt{mplusObject} method

You can change the model specification for LPA using the syntax provided in lecture.

\subsubsection{Model 1}\label{model-1-1}

When estimating LPA in Mplus, the default variance/covariance specification is the most restricted model (Model 1).
So we don't have to specify anything here.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{lpa\_k14  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lpa\_enum  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
      
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile \{k\}"}\NormalTok{), }
  
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"usevar = broad\_interest{-}self\_efficacy;}
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\}); "}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 500 100;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat svalues residual tech11 tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(pisa),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ pisa)}

\NormalTok{lpa\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(lpa\_enum, }
                \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lpa\_pisa"}\NormalTok{)),}
                \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_lpa\_m1.inp"}\NormalTok{)) ,}
                \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsubsection{Model 2}\label{model-2-1}

Here, an addition loop adds the variance/covariance specifications for each class-specific statement.
For the profile-varying/diagonal specification, you must specify the variances to be freely estimated:

\texttt{broad\_interest-self\_efficacy;}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lpa\_m2\_k14  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k)\{ }
  
  \CommentTok{\# This MODEL section changes the model specification}
\NormalTok{  MODEL }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{k, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
    \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}
\StringTok{    \%c\#\{i\}\%}
\StringTok{    broad\_interest{-}self\_efficacy;      ! variances are freely estimated}
\StringTok{    "}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  \}), }\AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  lpa\_enum\_m2  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile \{k\} {-} Model 2"}\NormalTok{),}
    
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"usevar = broad\_interest{-}self\_efficacy;}
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\});"}\NormalTok{),}
    
    \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
      \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 500 100;"}\NormalTok{,}
    
    \AttributeTok{MODEL =}\NormalTok{ MODEL,}
    
    
    \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat svalues residual tech11 tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}
    
    \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(pisa),}
    \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ pisa)}
  
\NormalTok{  lpa\_m2\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(lpa\_enum\_m2,}
                             \AttributeTok{dataout =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lpa\_pisa"}\NormalTok{),}
                             \AttributeTok{modelout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_lpa"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_lpa\_m2.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
                             \AttributeTok{check =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

For reference, here is the Mplus syntax for different specifications:

\textbf{Fixed covariance to zero (DEFAULT)}:

\texttt{broad\_interest\ WITH\ enjoyment@0;}

\textbf{Free covariance}:

\texttt{broad\_interest\ WITH\ enjoyment;}

\textbf{Equal covariances}:

\texttt{\%c\#1\%}

\texttt{broad\_interest\ WITH\ enjoyment\ (1);}

\texttt{\%c\#2\%}

\texttt{broad\_interest\ WITH\ enjoyment\ (1);}

\textbf{Equal variance (DEFAULT)}:

\texttt{\%c\#1\%}

\texttt{broad\_interest\ (1);}

\texttt{\%c\#2\%}

\texttt{broad\_interest\ (1);}

\textbf{Free variance}:

\texttt{mth\_scor-bio\_scor;}

You can also open the \texttt{tidyLPA} .inp files to see the specifications.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Table of Fit}\label{table-of-fit-2}

Evaluate each model specification separately using fit indices.

After examining the outputs (and increasing the random starts where necessary), there are a few models excluded from analysis:

Model 2:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Profile 4
\item
  Profile 5
\end{itemize}

Model 3:

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  Profile 5
\end{itemize}

Model 4:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Profile 4
\item
  Profile 5
\end{itemize}

Model 5:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Profile 3
\item
  Profile 4
\item
  Profile 5
\end{itemize}

Model 6:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Profile 4
\item
  Profile 5
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum\_table\_lpa.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Preview with numbered rows}
\FunctionTok{enum\_fit}\NormalTok{(output\_enum)}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} \# A tibble: 29 x 12}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}      row Title     Parameters     LL   BIC  aBIC  CAIC   AWE}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}    \textless{}int\textgreater{} \textless{}chr\textgreater{}          \textless{}dbl\textgreater{}  \textless{}dbl\textgreater{} \textless{}dbl\textgreater{} \textless{}dbl\textgreater{} \textless{}dbl\textgreater{} \textless{}dbl\textgreater{}}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  1     1 Model 1 \textasciitilde{}          8 {-}2089. 4227. 4201. 4235. 4300.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  2     2 Model 1 \textasciitilde{}         13 {-}1997. 4074. 4032. 4087. 4193.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  3     3 Model 1 \textasciitilde{}         18 {-}1953. 4017. 3960. 4035. 4183.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  4     4 Model 1 \textasciitilde{}         23 {-}1889. 3921. 3848. 3944. 4133.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  5     5 Model 1 \textasciitilde{}         28 {-}1871. 3915. 3826. 3943. 4172.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  6     6 Model 2 \textasciitilde{}          8 {-}2089. 4227. 4201. 4235. 4300.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  7     7 Model 2 \textasciitilde{}         17 {-}1989. 4083. 4029. 4100. 4239.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  8     8 Model 2 \textasciitilde{}         26 {-}1878. 3917. 3834. 3943. 4156.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  9     9 Model 2 \textasciitilde{}         35 {-}1851. 3919. 3808. 3954. 4241.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} 10    10 Model 2 \textasciitilde{}         44 {-}1825. 3923. 3783. 3967. 4327.}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} \# i 19 more rows}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} \# i 4 more variables: BLRT\_PValue \textless{}dbl\textgreater{},}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} \#   T11\_VLMR\_PValue \textless{}dbl\textgreater{}, BF \textless{}dbl\textgreater{}, cmPk \textless{}dbl\textgreater{}}


\NormalTok{select\_models }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\FunctionTok{LatexSummaryTable}\NormalTok{(output\_enum,                                 }
                \AttributeTok{keepCols=}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Title"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Parameters"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"LL"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{,}
                           \StringTok{"BLRT\_PValue"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"T11\_VLMR\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"Observations"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{slice}\NormalTok{( }\CommentTok{\# Remove the models that we don\textquotesingle{}t want to consider!!!! Because we looked at every single output, we know which models did not converge, thus we exclude them.}
    \CommentTok{\# Model 2}
    \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{9}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }
    \CommentTok{\# Model 3}
    \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{,}
    \CommentTok{\# Model 4}
    \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{19}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
    \CommentTok{\# Model 5}
    \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{23}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{24}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{25}\NormalTok{,}
    \CommentTok{\# Model 6}
    \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{29}\NormalTok{, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{30}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Check to make sure that the rows of the models we don\textquotesingle{}t want are removed}
\CommentTok{\#View(select\_models)}

\FunctionTok{enum\_table}\NormalTok{(select\_models, }\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{9}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{13}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{16}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{17}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{18}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\textsuperscript{\textit{1}}\emph{Note.} Par = Parameters; \emph{LL} = model log likelihood;
BIC = Bayesian information criterion;
aBIC = sample size adjusted BIC; CAIC = consistent Akaike information criterion;
AWE = approximate weight of evidence criterion;
BLRT = bootstrapped likelihood ratio test p-value;
cmPk = approximate correct model probability.\\
\end{minipage}
\end{table}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Information Criteria Plot}\label{information-criteria-plot-2}

Look for ``elbow'' to help with profile selection

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ic\_plot\_lpa.R"}\NormalTok{))}
\FunctionTok{ic\_plot}\NormalTok{(select\_models)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{07-lpa-enum_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-11-1.pdf}}

Based on fit indices, I am choosing the following candidate models:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  Model 1: 2 Profile
\item
  Model 2: 3 Profile
\item
  Model 3: 4 Profile
\item
  Model 4: 3 Profile
\item
  Model 5: 2 Profile
\item
  Model 6: 2 Profile
\end{enumerate}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Compare models}\label{compare-models}

\subsection{Correct Model Probability (cmpK) recalculation}\label{correct-model-probability-cmpk-recalculation}

Take the candidate models and recalculate the approximate correct model probabilities (Masyn, 2013)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# CmpK recalculation:}
\NormalTok{enum\_fit1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{enum\_fit}\NormalTok{(output\_enum)}

\NormalTok{stage2\_cmpk }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ enum\_fit1 }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{slice}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{14}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{18}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{22}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{27}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.}\DecValTok{5} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ BIC,}
         \AttributeTok{expSIC =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(SIC)),}
         \AttributeTok{cmPk =}\NormalTok{ expSIC }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(expSIC),}
         \AttributeTok{BF =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Title, Parameters, BIC}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{AWE, cmPk, BF)}

 
\CommentTok{\# Format Fit Table}
\NormalTok{stage2\_cmpk }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{column\_labels.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}
    \DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{drop\_trailing\_zeros =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{suffixing =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{3}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{),}
             \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}\AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{drop\_trailing\_zeros=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
             \AttributeTok{suffixing =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fns =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }
    \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x}\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textgreater{}100"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{           scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_style}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{style =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \FunctionTok{cell\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ),}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ BIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(BIC[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(stage2\_cmpk)]) }
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ aBIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ aBIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(aBIC[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(stage2\_cmpk)])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ CAIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ CAIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(CAIC[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(stage2\_cmpk)])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ AWE,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ AWE }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(AWE[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(stage2\_cmpk)])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ cmPk,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{  cmPk }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(cmPk[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(stage2\_cmpk)])}
\NormalTok{     ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BF, }
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{  BF }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Compare loglikelihood}\label{compare-loglikelihood}

You can also compare models using nested model testing directly with MplusAutomation.
Note that you can only compare across models but the profiles must stay the same.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# MplusAutomation Method using \textasciigrave{}compareModels\textasciigrave{} }

\FunctionTok{compareModels}\NormalTok{(output\_enum[[}\StringTok{"model\_2\_class\_3.out"}\NormalTok{]],}
\NormalTok{  output\_enum[[}\StringTok{"model\_4\_class\_3.out"}\NormalTok{]], }\AttributeTok{diffTest =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} ==============}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Mplus model comparison}

\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Model 1:  C:\textbackslash{}Users\textbackslash{}dnajiarch\textbackslash{}Box\textbackslash{}lca{-}bookdown\textbackslash{}lpa\textbackslash{}tidyLPA/model\_2\_class\_3.out }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Model 2:  C:\textbackslash{}Users\textbackslash{}dnajiarch\textbackslash{}Box\textbackslash{}lca{-}bookdown\textbackslash{}lpa\textbackslash{}tidyLPA/model\_4\_class\_3.out }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Model Summary Comparison}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} {-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}{-}}

\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Title        model 2 with 3 classes model 4 with 3 classes}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Observations 488                    488                   }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Estimator    MLR                    MLR                   }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} Parameters   26                     32                    }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} LL           {-}1877.965              {-}1859.466             }

\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  BROAD\_IN.WITH  ENJOYMENT                            2}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  BROAD\_IN.WITH  ENJOYMENT                            3}

\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Here, Model 1 (restricted, fewer parameters) is nested in Model 2.
The chi-square difference test, assuming nested models, shows a significant improvement in fit for Model 2 over Model 1, despite the added parameters.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\chapter{Item Mean Plots}\label{item-mean-plots}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\emph{Example: PISA Student Data}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  The first example closely follows the vignette used to demonstrate the \href{https://data-edu.github.io/tidyLPA/articles/Introduction_to_tidyLPA.html}{tidyLPA} package (Rosenberg, 2019).
\end{enumerate}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  This model utilizes the \texttt{PISA} data collected in the U.S. in 2015. To learn more about this data \href{http://www.oecd.org/pisa/data/}{see here}.
\item
  To access the 2015 US \texttt{PISA} data \& documentation in R use the following code:
\end{itemize}

Variables:

\begin{description}
\item[\texttt{broad\_interest}]
composite measure of students' self reported broad interest
\item[\texttt{enjoyment}]
composite measure of students' self reported enjoyment
\item[\texttt{instrumental\_mot}]
composite measure of students' self reported instrumental motivation
\item[\texttt{self\_efficacy}]
composite measure of students' self reported self efficacy
\end{description}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\#devtools::install\_github("jrosen48/pisaUSA15")}
\CommentTok{\#library(pisaUSA15)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load packages}\label{load-packages-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(naniar)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyLPA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(pisaUSA15)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(filesstrings)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(patchwork)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(RcppAlgos)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Prepare Data}\label{prepare-data-5}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{pisa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pisaUSA15[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,] }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(broad\_interest, enjoyment, instrumental\_mot, self\_efficacy)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  3rd Qu.:3.200   3rd Qu.:3.00   3rd Qu.:2.500   }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  Max.   :5.000   Max.   :4.00   Max.   :4.000   }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  NA\textquotesingle{}s   :23      NA\textquotesingle{}s   :14     NA\textquotesingle{}s   :21      }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  self\_efficacy  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  Min.   :1.000  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  1st Qu.:1.750  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  Median :2.000  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  Mean   :2.125  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  3rd Qu.:2.500  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  Max.   :4.000  }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  NA\textquotesingle{}s   :23}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Mean Table

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ds }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pisa }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(broad\_interest}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{self\_efficacy, }\AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
            \AttributeTok{sd =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(value, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)) }

\NormalTok{ds }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{ () }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Descriptive Summary**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{variable =} \StringTok{"Variable"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{mean =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"M"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{sd =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"SD"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{),}
             \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ mean}
\NormalTok{  ) }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Histograms

  \FunctionTok{geom\_histogram}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{binwidth =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{fill =} \StringTok{"\#69b3a2"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}

  \FunctionTok{theme\_cowplot}\NormalTok{()}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lpa.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Read in models}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lpa}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_3\_class\_4.out)}
\end{Highlighting}

\end{Highlighting}

  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model 3: Equal Variances, Equal Covariances"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}

\subsection{Plot comparison}\label{plot-comparison}

We can also plot the comparisons and look at the error bars.

NOTE: The \texttt{plotMixtures()} function is used for plotting LPA models only (i.e., means \& variances)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{a }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plotMixtures}\NormalTok{(output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_2\_class\_3.out,}
  \AttributeTok{ci =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bw =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }

\NormalTok{b }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{plotMixtures}\NormalTok{(output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_4\_class\_3.out,}
  \AttributeTok{ci =} \FloatTok{0.95}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{bw =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{) }

\NormalTok{a }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model 2"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
\NormalTok{b }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model 4"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
    \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{08-lpa-plot_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-10-1.pdf}}

\part{Understanding the Data}\label{part-understanding-the-data}

\chapter{Observed Response Patterns}\label{observed-response-patterns}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load Packages}\label{load-packages-2}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(naniar)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyLPA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(pisaUSA15)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(filesstrings)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(patchwork)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(RcppAlgos)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Prepare Data}\label{prepare-data-6}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{df\_bully }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"crdc\_lca\_data.csv"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(report\_dis, report\_race, report\_sex, counselors\_fte, psych\_fte, law\_fte) }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Continuing the LCA example (\ref{lca-enum}) in this bookdown, save response frequencies for the 3-class model with \texttt{response\ is\ \_\_\_\_\_.dat} under \texttt{SAVEDATA.}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{patterns  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"C3 LCA {-} Save response patterns"}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = report\_dis{-}law\_fte; }
\StringTok{   usevar =  report\_dis{-}law\_fte;}
\StringTok{   classes = c(3);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 0;}
\StringTok{    processors = 10;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 802779;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File=savedata.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save=cprob;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    ! Code to save response frequency data }
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    response is resp\_patterns.dat;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"residual patterns tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(df\_bully),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ df\_bully)}

\NormalTok{patterns\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(patterns,}
                \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bully.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"patterns.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Read in observed response pattern data and relabel the columns

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in response frequency data that we just created:}
\NormalTok{patterns }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_table}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"resp\_patterns.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                        \AttributeTok{col\_names=}\ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{na =} \StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{) }

\CommentTok{\# Extract the column names}
\NormalTok{names }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"patterns.out"}\NormalTok{))[[}\StringTok{\textquotesingle{}savedata\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{]]) }

\CommentTok{\# Add the names back to the dataset}
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(patterns) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Frequency"}\NormalTok{, names) }

\CommentTok{\# Rename the variable }
\NormalTok{patterns }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ patterns }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{C =}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{any\_of}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"MLCC"}\NormalTok{)))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Create a table with the top 5 unconditional response pattern, then top of conditional response pattern for each modal class assignment

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Order responses by highest frequency}
\NormalTok{order\_highest }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ patterns }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{C =}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{any\_of}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"MLCC"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{desc}\NormalTok{(Frequency))}

\CommentTok{\# Loop \textasciigrave{}patterns\textasciigrave{} data to list top 5 conditional response patterns for each class}
\NormalTok{loop\_cond  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(patterns}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{C), }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{       }
\NormalTok{order\_cond }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ patterns }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                    
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(C }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                    
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{desc}\NormalTok{(Frequency)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                
  \FunctionTok{head}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{)                                     }
\NormalTok{  \})                                          }

\CommentTok{\# Convert loop into data frame}
\NormalTok{table\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{(loop\_cond))}

\CommentTok{\# Combine unconditional and conditional responses patterns}
\NormalTok{response\_patterns }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}  \FunctionTok{rbind}\NormalTok{(order\_highest[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{,], table\_data) }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Finally, use \texttt{\{gt\}} to make a nicely formatted table

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{resp\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ response\_patterns }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Observed Response Patterns"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Response patterns, estimated frequencies, estimated posterior class probabilities and modal assignments"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{tab\_source\_note}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{source\_note =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Data Source: **Civil Rights Data Collection (CRDC)**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{Frequency =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\textless{}i\textgreater{}f\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}sub\textgreater{}r\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{REPORT\_D =} \StringTok{"Harrassment: Disability"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{REPORT\_R =} \StringTok{"Harrassment: Race"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{REPORT\_S =} \StringTok{"Harrassment: Sex"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{COUNSELO =} \StringTok{"Staff: Counselor"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{PSYCH\_FT =} \StringTok{"Staff: Psychologist"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{LAW\_FTE =} \StringTok{"Staff: Law Enforcement"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{CPROB1 =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"P\textless{}sub\textgreater{}\textless{}i\textgreater{}k\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}=1"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{CPROB2 =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"P\textless{}sub\textgreater{}\textless{}i\textgreater{}k\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}=2"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{CPROB3 =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"P\textless{}sub\textgreater{}\textless{}i\textgreater{}k\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}=3"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{C =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k*"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_row\_group}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Unconditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{rows =} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_row\_group}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 1 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{rows =} \DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \CommentTok{\#EDIT THESE VALUES BASED ON THE LAST COLUMN}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_row\_group}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 2 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{rows =} \DecValTok{11}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{15}\NormalTok{)  }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \CommentTok{\#EDIT THESE VALUES BASED ON THE LAST COLUMN}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_row\_group}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 3 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{rows =} \DecValTok{16}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)  }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \CommentTok{\#EDIT THESE VALUES BASED ON THE LAST COLUMN  }
    \FunctionTok{row\_group\_order}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{groups =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Unconditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{,}
                 \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 1 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{),}
                 \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 2 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{),}
                 \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k* = 3 Conditional response patterns"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"\textless{}i\textgreater{}Note.\textless{}/i\textgreater{} \textless{}i\textgreater{}f\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}sub\textgreater{}r\textless{}/sub\textgreater{} = response pattern frequency; P\textless{}sub\textgreater{}\textless{}i\textgreater{}k\textless{}/i\textgreater{}\textless{}/sub\textgreater{} = posterior class probabilities"}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{opt\_align\_table\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  gt}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.font.names =} \StringTok{"Times New Roman"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{resp\_table}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Save table:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{gtsave}\NormalTok{(resp\_table, }\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"figures"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"resp\_table.png"}\NormalTok{))}

\chapter{Classification Diagnostics}\label{classification-diagnostics}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load Packages}\label{load-packages-3}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(naniar)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyLPA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(pisaUSA15)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(filesstrings)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(patchwork)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(RcppAlgos)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{LCA}\label{lca}

Prepare Data

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{df\_bully }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"crdc\_lca\_data.csv"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(report\_dis, report\_race, report\_sex, counselors\_fte, psych\_fte, law\_fte) }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Continuing the LCA example (\ref{lca-enum}) in this bookdown, use Mplus to calculate k-class confidence intervals (Note: Change the syntax to make your chosen \emph{k}-class model):

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{classification  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"C3 LCA {-} Calculated k{-}Class 95\% CI"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =}
    \StringTok{"categorical = report\_dis{-}law\_fte;}
\StringTok{   usevar =  report\_dis{-}law\_fte;}
\StringTok{   classes = c(3);"}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =}
    \StringTok{"estimator = ml;}
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 0; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 802779;}
\StringTok{    bootstrap = 1000;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
    \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{  !CHANGE THIS SECTION TO YOUR CHOSEN k{-}CLASS MODEL}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{  \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{  [C\#1](c1);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [C\#2](C2);}

\StringTok{  Model Constraint:}
\StringTok{  New(p1 p2 p3);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  p1 = exp(c1)/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2));}
\StringTok{  p2 = exp(c2)/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2));}
\StringTok{  p3 = 1/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2));"}\NormalTok{,}

  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"cinterval(bcbootstrap)"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(df\_bully),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ df\_bully)}

\NormalTok{classification\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(classification,}
                \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"bully.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"class.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\emph{Note}: Ensure that the classes did not shift during this step (i.g., Class 1 in the enumeration run is now Class 4). Evaluate output and compare the class counts and proportions for the latent classes. Using the OPTSEED function ensures replication of the best loglikelihood value run.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Read in the 3-class model:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in the 3{-}class model and extract information needed}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"class.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# 95\% k{-}Class and k{-}class 95\% Confidence Intervals}
\NormalTok{k\_ci }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{ci.unstandardized }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(paramHeader }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"New.Additional.Parameters"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{unite}\NormalTok{(CI, }\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(low2}\FloatTok{.5}\NormalTok{,up2}\FloatTok{.5}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sep=}\StringTok{", "}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{remove =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CI =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"["}\NormalTok{, CI, }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{kclass=}\NormalTok{est) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(kclass, CI)}

\CommentTok{\# AvePPk = Average Latent Class Probabilities for Most Likely Latent Class Membership (Row) by Latent Class (Column)}
\NormalTok{avePPk }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{avePPk =} \FunctionTok{diag}\NormalTok{(output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{avgProbs.mostLikely))}

\CommentTok{\# mcaPk = modal class assignment proportion }
\NormalTok{mcaPk }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mostLikely,}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Class "}\NormalTok{, class)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{add\_column}\NormalTok{(avePPk, k\_ci) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mcaPk =}\NormalTok{ proportion) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(model, kclass, CI, mcaPk, avePPk)}

\CommentTok{\# OCCk = odds of correct classification}
\NormalTok{OCCk }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ mcaPk }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{OCCk =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{((avePPk}\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{avePPk))}\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{(kclass}\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{kclass)),}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Put everything together}
\NormalTok{class\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(OCCk)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Now, use \texttt{\{gt\}} to make a nicely formatted table

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{class\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ class\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Model Classification Diagnostics for the 3{-}Class Solution"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{model =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k*{-}Class"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{kclass =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*k*{-}Class Proportions"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{CI =} \StringTok{"95\% CI"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{mcaPk =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"McaP\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{avePPk =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"AvePP\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{OCCk =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"OCC\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{}"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \FunctionTok{html}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"\textless{}i\textgreater{}Note.\textless{}/i\textgreater{} McaP\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{} = Modal class assignment proportion; AvePP\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{} = Average posterior class probabilities; OCC\textless{}sub\textgreater{}k\textless{}/sub\textgreater{} = Odds of correct classification; "}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{opt\_align\_table\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  gt}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.font.names =} \StringTok{"Times New Roman"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{class\_table}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large Model Classification Diagnostics for the 3-Class Solution}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{\linewidth}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}cccccc}
\toprule
\emph{k}-Class & \emph{k}-Class Proportions & 95\% CI & McaP<sub>k</sub> & AvePPk & OCCk \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
Class 1 & 0.249 & [0.166, 0.329] & 0.282 & 0.675 & 6.264 \\ 
Class 2 & 0.106 & [0.083, 0.136] & 0.095 & 0.904 & 79.420 \\ 
Class 3 & 0.644 & [0.561, 0.731] & 0.623 & 0.893 & 4.614 \\ 
\bottomrule
\end{tabular*}
\begin{minipage}{\linewidth}
<i>Note.</i> McaP<sub>k</sub> = Modal class assignment proportion; AvePP<sub>k</sub> = Average posterior class probabilities; OCC<sub>k</sub> = Odds of correct classification; \\
\end{minipage}
\end{table}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{LPA}\label{lpa}

Prepare Data

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{pisa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pisaUSA15[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,] }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(broad\_interest, enjoyment, instrumental\_mot, self\_efficacy)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Continuing the LPA example (\ref{lpa-enum}) in this bookdown, use Mplus to calculate k-class confidence intervals (Note: Change the syntax to make your chosen \emph{k}-class model):

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{classification  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"LPA {-} Calculated k{-}Class 95\% CI"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"usevar =  broad\_interest{-}self\_efficacy;}
\StringTok{   classes = c1(4);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = ml; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;    }
\StringTok{    starts = 0; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 468036; ! This seed is taken from chosen model output}
\StringTok{    bootstrap = 1000;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
    \StringTok{" }
\StringTok{    ! This is copied and pasted from the chosen model input}
\StringTok{  \%c1\#1\%}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest (vbroad\_interest);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment (venjoyment);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot (vinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  self\_efficacy (vself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH enjoyment (broad\_interestWenjoyment);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH instrumental\_mot (broad\_interestWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH self\_efficacy (broad\_interestWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH instrumental\_mot (enjoymentWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH self\_efficacy (enjoymentWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot WITH self\_efficacy (instrumental\_motWself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  \%c1\#2\%}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest (vbroad\_interest);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment (venjoyment);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot (vinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  self\_efficacy (vself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH enjoyment (broad\_interestWenjoyment);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH instrumental\_mot (broad\_interestWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH self\_efficacy (broad\_interestWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH instrumental\_mot (enjoymentWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH self\_efficacy (enjoymentWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot WITH self\_efficacy (instrumental\_motWself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  \%c1\#3\%}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest (vbroad\_interest);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment (venjoyment);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot (vinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  self\_efficacy (vself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH enjoyment (broad\_interestWenjoyment);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH instrumental\_mot (broad\_interestWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH self\_efficacy (broad\_interestWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH instrumental\_mot (enjoymentWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH self\_efficacy (enjoymentWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot WITH self\_efficacy (instrumental\_motWself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  \%c1\#4\%}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest (vbroad\_interest);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment (venjoyment);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot (vinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  self\_efficacy (vself\_efficacy);}

\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH enjoyment (broad\_interestWenjoyment);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH instrumental\_mot (broad\_interestWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  broad\_interest WITH self\_efficacy (broad\_interestWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH instrumental\_mot (enjoymentWinstrumental\_mot);}
\StringTok{  enjoyment WITH self\_efficacy (enjoymentWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  instrumental\_mot WITH self\_efficacy (instrumental\_motWself\_efficacy);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  !CHANGE THIS SECTION TO YOUR CHOSEN k{-}CLASS MODEL}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{  \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{  [C1\#1](c1);}
\StringTok{  [C1\#2](c2);}
\StringTok{  [C1\#3](c3);}

\StringTok{  Model Constraint:}
\StringTok{  New(p1 p2 p3 p4);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  p1 = exp(c1)/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2)+exp(c3));}
\StringTok{  p2 = exp(c2)/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2)+exp(c3));}
\StringTok{  p3 = exp(c3)/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2)+exp(c3));  }
\StringTok{  p4 = 1/(1+exp(c1)+exp(c2)+exp(c3));"}\NormalTok{,}

  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"cinterval(bcbootstrap)"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(pisa),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ pisa)}

\NormalTok{classification\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(classification,}
                \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"class.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"class.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Create table using \texttt{diagnostics\_table} function

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"diagnostics\_table.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{class\_output }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"mplus"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"class.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{diagnostics\_table}\NormalTok{(class\_output)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]

{\large Model Classification Diagnostics}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont

\toprule
\emph{k}-Class & \emph{k}-Class Proportions & 95\% CI & \emph{mcaPk} & \emph{AvePPk} & \emph{OCCk} \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
