\chapter{Class Homogeneity}\label{class-homogeneity}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load packages}\label{load-packages-4}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(naniar)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyLPA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(pisaUSA15)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(filesstrings)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(patchwork)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(RcppAlgos)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{LCA}\label{lca-1}

Continuing the LCA example (\ref{lca-enum}) in this bookdown, use R to evaluate the LCA class homogeneity.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

We want a high degree of homogeneity (small variance) within each class (i.e., people in each class are not identical but are more similar to each other than to people in different classes).

Class-specific response probabilities above .70 or below .30 indicate high homogeneity in that particular class for that specific item. For a binary variable, \(X\), \(Var(X) = p(1-p)\). The largest variance possible for a binary variable is when \(p = .5\), the smallest variance (0) is when \(p = 0\) or \(p = 1\).

The easiest way is to visualize the item probability plot:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lca.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"bully"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Change this to look at your chosen LCA model}
\NormalTok{chosen\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c4\_bully.out}

\CommentTok{\# Add lines}
\FunctionTok{plot\_lca}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FunctionTok{geom\_hline}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{yintercept =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\includegraphics[width=0.9\linewidth]{11-homogeneity_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-2-1}

Or create a table:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"bully"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Change this to look at your chosen LCA model}
\NormalTok{chosen\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c4\_bully.out}

\CommentTok{\# Extract table of probabilities}
\NormalTok{probabilities }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{probability.scale) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{LatentClass =} \FunctionTok{sub}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\^{}"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Class "}\NormalTok{, LatentClass)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(category }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(est, LatentClass, param) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ est) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{relocate}\NormalTok{(param, }\AttributeTok{.after =} \FunctionTok{last\_col}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Items =}\NormalTok{ param)}

\CommentTok{\# Bold above .7 and below .3}
\NormalTok{bold\_condition }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) \{}
  \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{0.7} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.3}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x), }\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{),}
         \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Apply the bold formatting condition to the table}
\NormalTok{homogeneity }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ probabilities }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{across}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{where}\NormalTok{(is.numeric), }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(., bold\_condition))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{relocate}\NormalTok{(Items, }\AttributeTok{.before =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)}


\CommentTok{\# Display the formatted table and percentage}
\NormalTok{homogeneity }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{()  }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Items =} \StringTok{"Item"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{3}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Class Homogeneity Table"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_markdown}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Cells in bold indicate probabilities \textgreater{} 0.7 or \textless{} 0.3"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large Class Homogeneity Table}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{\linewidth}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}lrrrr}
\toprule
Item & Class 1 & Class 2 & Class 3 & Class 4 \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
REPORT\_DIS & 0.314 & 0.016 & 0.016 & 0.000 \\ 
REPORT\_RAC & 0.797 & 0 & 0.018 & 0.045 \\ 
REPORT\_SEX & 0.698 & 0.204 & 0.025 & 0.104 \\ 
COUNSELORS & 0.574 & 1 & 0.384 & 0.000 \\ 
PSYCH\_FTE & 0.669 & 0.757 & 0.009 & 0.630 \\ 
LAW\_FTE & 0.267 & 0.256 & 0 & 0.087 \\ 
\bottomrule
\end{tabular*}
\begin{minipage}{\linewidth}
Cells in bold indicate probabilities > 0.7 or < 0.3\\
\end{minipage}
\end{table}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{LPA}\label{lpa-1}

Continuing the LPA example (\ref{lpa-enum}) in this bookdown, use R to evaluate the LPA profile homogeneity.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Profile homogeneity is the model-estimated within-profile variances for each indicator m across the \emph{k}-profiles and comparing them to the total overall sample variance:

\[ \frac{\theta_{mk}}{\theta_{m}} \]

Where \(\theta{mk}\) is the item variance for item \(m\), class \(k\) and \(\theta{m}\) is the item variance for item \(m\) not conditioned on class.

Individuals belonging to the same class are more similar to other members of that class than they are compared to members of other classes. Individuals belonging to the same class are closer to the class mean than they are to the overall population mean. Within-class variance for each indicator is smaller than overall population variance, therefore:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Values \textgreater{} .90 corresponds to a high degree of homogeneity
\item
  Values \textless{} .60 corresponds to a low degree of homogeneity
\end{itemize}

To calculate the values in a table:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Change this to look at your chosen LPA model}
\NormalTok{chosen\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_3\_class\_4.out}

\CommentTok{\# Extract overall and profile{-}specific variances}
\NormalTok{overall\_variances }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{sampstat}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{univariate.sample.statistics) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Variance) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rownames\_to\_column}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{var =} \StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =} \FunctionTok{str\_sub}\NormalTok{(item, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{profile\_variances }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{unstandardized) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(paramHeader }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"Variances"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, est, LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =}\NormalTok{ param,}
         \AttributeTok{variance =}\NormalTok{ est,}
         \AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile "}\NormalTok{, k)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =} \FunctionTok{str\_sub}\NormalTok{(item, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{))}


\CommentTok{\# Bold above 0.90 and italicize below 0.60}
\NormalTok{format\_condition }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) \{}
  \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \FloatTok{0.9}\NormalTok{,}
         \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x), }\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{),   }\CommentTok{\# bold}
         \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.6}\NormalTok{,}
                \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x), }\StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{),  }\CommentTok{\# italic}
                \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x)))  }\CommentTok{\# normal}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Evaluate the ratio}
\NormalTok{homogeneity }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ profile\_variances }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(overall\_variances, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{homogeneity\_ratio =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{((variance.x }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ variance.y),}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{across}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{where}\NormalTok{(is.numeric), }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{as.character}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(., format\_condition))))}

\CommentTok{\# Create a gt table}
\NormalTok{homogeneity }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(homogeneity\_ratio, k, item) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ k, }
    \AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ homogeneity\_ratio}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{item =} \StringTok{"Item"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{3}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Profile Homogeneity Table"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Cells in bold indicate proportions \textgreater{} 0.90 and cells in italics indicate proportions \textless{} 0.60. }
\StringTok{    Note: In this example, the class variances are set to be equal."}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_markdown}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{())}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large Profile Homogeneity Table}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{\linewidth}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}lrrrr}
\toprule
Item & Profile 1 & Profile 2 & Profile 3 & Profile 4 \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
BROAD\_IN & \textbf{0.970} & \textbf{0.970} & \textbf{0.970} & \textbf{0.970} \\ 
ENJOYMEN & \textbf{0.930} & \textbf{0.930} & \textbf{0.930} & \textbf{0.930} \\ 
INSTRUME & \emph{0.060} & \emph{0.060} & \emph{0.060} & \emph{0.060} \\ 
SELF\_EFF & \textbf{0.950} & \textbf{0.950} & \textbf{0.950} & \textbf{0.950} \\ 
\bottomrule
\end{tabular*}
\begin{minipage}{\linewidth}
Cells in bold indicate proportions > 0.90 and cells in italics indicate proportions < 0.60. 
    Note: In this example, the class variances are set to be equal.\\
\end{minipage}
\end{table}

You can also visualize the plot:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lpa.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lpa}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_3\_class\_4.out)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\includegraphics[width=0.9\linewidth]{11-homogeneity_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-5-1}

\chapter{Class Separation}\label{class-separation}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load Packages}\label{load-packages-5}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(naniar)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyLPA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(pisaUSA15)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(filesstrings)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(patchwork)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(RcppAlgos)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{LCA}\label{lca-2}

Continuing the LCA example (\ref{lca-enum}) in this bookdown, use R to evaluate the LCA class separation.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\[\widehat{OR_{1v2}} = \frac{\frac{P(u_1=1|c_1)}{P(u_1=0|c_1)}}{\frac{P(u_1=1|c_2)}{P(u_1=0|c_2)}} \]

Odds ratios (ORs) of item endorsements between two classes \textgreater5 (corresponding to class-specific endorsement probabilities of \textgreater.7 and \textless.3) or \textless.2 (corresponding to class-specific endorsement probabilities of \textless.3 and \textgreater.7), indicate high separation between those two classes on that specific item. No single binary (0/1) indicator can separate more than two subsets of latent classes, no matter what the total number of classes in the model. We want a high degree of separation (large standardized mean differences) between classes.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"bully"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Change this to look at your chosen LCA model}
\NormalTok{chosen\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c4\_bully.out}

\CommentTok{\# Extract table of probabilities}
\NormalTok{c\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{probability.scale) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{LatentClass =} \FunctionTok{sub}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\^{}"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Class "}\NormalTok{, LatentClass)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(category }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(est, LatentClass, param) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =}\NormalTok{ param, }\AttributeTok{prob =}\NormalTok{ est, }\AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\CommentTok{\# Adjust probabilities by setting a minimum of 0.01 and a maximum of 0.99}
    \AttributeTok{prob =} \FunctionTok{pmin}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{pmax}\NormalTok{(prob, }\FloatTok{0.01}\NormalTok{), }\FloatTok{0.99}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{prob =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(prob))}


\CommentTok{\# Relative class sizes}
\NormalTok{class\_sizes }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{modelEstimated) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =}\NormalTok{ proportion, }\AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ class) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Class "}\NormalTok{, k)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{count)}


\CommentTok{\# Create the combinations of classes}
\NormalTok{class\_combinations }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{comboGrid}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{k1 =} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(class\_sizes}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k),}
  \AttributeTok{k2 =} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(class\_sizes}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k)}
\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k1 }\SpecialCharTok{!=}\NormalTok{ k2) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(k1, k2)}

\CommentTok{\# Calculate pairwise comparisons for probabilities (e.g., P(u1=1|c1) / P(u1=0|c1) for each combination)}
\NormalTok{combined\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ class\_combinations }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rowwise}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{do}\NormalTok{(\{}
\NormalTok{    pair }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ .}
\NormalTok{    k1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pair}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k1}
\NormalTok{    k2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pair}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k2}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Filter for the probabilities (P(u1=1|c1) and P(u1=0|c1))}
\NormalTok{    data\_k1\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ c\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k1)}
\NormalTok{    data\_k2\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ c\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k2)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Combine the probability data for the two profiles}
\NormalTok{    prob\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data\_k1\_prob }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{inner\_join}\NormalTok{(data\_k2\_prob,}
                 \AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{,}
                 \AttributeTok{suffix =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\_k1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\_k2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{prob\_ratio =}\NormalTok{ (prob\_k1 }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ prob\_k1)) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (prob\_k2 }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ prob\_k2)),}
        \CommentTok{\# Division formula}
        \AttributeTok{comparison =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(k1, }\StringTok{"vs"}\NormalTok{, k2)}
\NormalTok{      ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prob\_ratio =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(}
        \FunctionTok{is.infinite}\NormalTok{(prob\_ratio) }\SpecialCharTok{|} \FunctionTok{is.nan}\NormalTok{(prob\_ratio),}
        \ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{        prob\_ratio}
\NormalTok{      ), }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{      dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(item, prob\_ratio, comparison)}
    
\NormalTok{    prob\_data}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{()}


\CommentTok{\# Define a function to apply the Markdown bold formatting}
\NormalTok{bold\_condition }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) \{}
  \CommentTok{\# If prob\_ratio is \textgreater{} 5 or \textless{} 0.2, add **}
  \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{5} \SpecialCharTok{|}\NormalTok{ x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x), }\StringTok{"**"}\NormalTok{),}
         \FunctionTok{sprintf}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%.3f"}\NormalTok{, x))}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Assuming combined\_results is already created as mentioned}
\NormalTok{formatted\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ combined\_results }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(prob\_ratio, comparison, item) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ comparison, }\AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ prob\_ratio) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{across}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{where}\NormalTok{(is.numeric), }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(., bold\_condition)))}

\CommentTok{\# Create a gt table}
\NormalTok{formatted\_results }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =} \StringTok{"Item"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_missing}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{missing\_text =} \StringTok{"{-}"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Class Separation Table"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_markdown}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Cells in bold indicate probabilities \textgreater{} 5 or \textless{} 0.2."}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

}

\section{LPA}\label{lpa-2}

Continuing the LPA example (\ref{lpa-enum}) in this bookdown, use R to evaluate the LPA profile separation.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

You can evaluate the degree of profile separation by assessing the actual distance between the profile-specific means. To quantify profile separation between Profile j and Profile k with respect to a particular item m, compute a standard mean difference:

\[\hat{d}_{mjk}= \frac{{\hat{\alpha_{mj}}-\hat{\alpha_{mk}}}}{\sigma_{mjk}}\] Pooled variance:

\[\hat{\sigma}_{mj k} = \sqrt{\frac{(\hat{\pi}_j)(n)(\hat{\theta}_{mj}) + (\hat{\pi}_k)(n)(\hat{\theta}_{mk})}{(\hat{\pi}_j + \hat{\pi}_k) n}}\]

Well-separated classes have a small degree of overlap of the class-specific indicator distributions; that is, standardized mean difference is large.

\begin{itemize}
\item
  A mean difference \textless.85 corresponds to low separation---more than 50\% overlap
\item
  A mean difference \textgreater{} 2 corresponds to high separation---less than 20\% overlap
\end{itemize}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Read in model}
\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Change this to look at your chosen LPA model}
\NormalTok{chosen\_model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_3\_class\_4.out}

\CommentTok{\# Profile{-}specific means}
\NormalTok{profile\_means }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{unstandardized) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(paramHeader }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"Means"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"\#"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, est, LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =}\NormalTok{ param,}
         \AttributeTok{means =}\NormalTok{ est,}
         \AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile "}\NormalTok{, k)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =} \FunctionTok{str\_sub}\NormalTok{(item, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Relative profile sizes}
\NormalTok{profile\_sizes }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{modelEstimated) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =}\NormalTok{ proportion,}
         \AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ class) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile "}\NormalTok{, k)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{count)}

\CommentTok{\# Sample size}
\NormalTok{n }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{summaries}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Observations}

\CommentTok{\# Profile{-}specific variance}
\NormalTok{profile\_variances }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(chosen\_model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{unstandardized) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(paramHeader }\SpecialCharTok{==} \StringTok{"Variances"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, est, LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =}\NormalTok{ param,}
         \AttributeTok{variance =}\NormalTok{ est,}
         \AttributeTok{k =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Profile "}\NormalTok{, k)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{item =} \FunctionTok{str\_sub}\NormalTok{(item, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Combine profile variances with profile sizes}
\NormalTok{variance\_with\_sizes }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ profile\_variances }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(profile\_sizes, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"k"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Create the combinations}
\NormalTok{profile\_combinations }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{comboGrid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{k1 =} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(profile\_sizes}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k), }\AttributeTok{k2 =} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(profile\_sizes}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k1 }\SpecialCharTok{!=}\NormalTok{ k2) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}  
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(k1, k2)}

\CommentTok{\# Calculate pooled variance for each item across the profiles}
\NormalTok{combined\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ profile\_combinations }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rowwise}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{do}\NormalTok{(\{}
\NormalTok{    pair }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ .}
\NormalTok{    k1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pair}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k1}
\NormalTok{    k2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ pair}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{k2}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Filter for the two profiles (variance)}
\NormalTok{    data\_k1\_var }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ variance\_with\_sizes }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k1)}
\NormalTok{    data\_k2\_var }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ variance\_with\_sizes }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k2)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Filter for the two profiles (means)}
\NormalTok{    data\_k1\_mean }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ profile\_means }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k1)}
\NormalTok{    data\_k2\_mean }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ profile\_means }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(k }\SpecialCharTok{==}\NormalTok{ k2)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Combine variance data for the two profiles}
\NormalTok{    variance\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data\_k1\_var }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{inner\_join}\NormalTok{(data\_k2\_var, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{suffix =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\_k1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\_k2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{pooled\_variance =} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{          ((size\_k1 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ variance\_k1) }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ (size\_k2 }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ variance\_k2)) }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ ((size\_k1 }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ size\_k2) }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ n)}
\NormalTok{        ),}
        \AttributeTok{comparison =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(k1, }\StringTok{"vs"}\NormalTok{, k2)}
\NormalTok{      ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{      dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(item, pooled\_variance, comparison)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Combine mean data for the two profilees}
\NormalTok{    mean\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ data\_k1\_mean }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{inner\_join}\NormalTok{(data\_k2\_mean, }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{suffix =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\_k1"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\_k2"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{mean\_diff =}\NormalTok{ means\_k1 }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ means\_k2,}
        \AttributeTok{comparison =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(k1, }\StringTok{"vs"}\NormalTok{, k2)}
\NormalTok{      ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{      dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(item, mean\_diff, comparison)}
    
    \CommentTok{\# Combine both variance and mean differences data}
\NormalTok{    combined\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ variance\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(mean\_data, }\AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"item"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"comparison"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
      \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
         \AttributeTok{mean\_diff\_by\_pooled\_variance =}\NormalTok{ mean\_diff }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ pooled\_variance}
\NormalTok{      )}
    
\NormalTok{    combined\_data}
\NormalTok{  \}) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{bind\_rows}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mean\_diff\_by\_pooled\_variance =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(mean\_diff\_by\_pooled\_variance, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(mean\_diff\_by\_pooled\_variance, comparison, item) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_wider}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{names\_from =}\NormalTok{ comparison, }
    \AttributeTok{values\_from =}\NormalTok{ mean\_diff\_by\_pooled\_variance}
\NormalTok{  )}
  

\CommentTok{\# Create a gt table}
\NormalTok{gt\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ combined\_results }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{item =} \StringTok{"Item"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Profile Separation Table"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Green cells indicate \textgreater{}2; Red cells indicate \textless{}0.85."}
\NormalTok{  )}


\CommentTok{\# Formatting thresholds}
\NormalTok{high\_threshold }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{low\_threshold }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FloatTok{0.85}


\CommentTok{\# Apply conditional colors for each numeric cell}
\NormalTok{num\_cols }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ gt\_table}\SpecialCharTok{$}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{\_data}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}\NormalTok{ dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{item) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{()  }\CommentTok{\# numeric columns}
\ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{(col }\ControlFlowTok{in}\NormalTok{ num\_cols)\{}
  \ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{(i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(combined\_results))\{}
\NormalTok{    val }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ combined\_results[[col]][i]  }\CommentTok{\# numeric value}
\NormalTok{    color }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{(val }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\StringTok{"\#66BB6A"} \ControlFlowTok{else} \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{(val }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.85}\NormalTok{) }\StringTok{"\#E57373"} \ControlFlowTok{else} \ConstantTok{NA}
    \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(color))\{}
\NormalTok{      gt\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ gt\_table }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
        \FunctionTok{tab\_style}\NormalTok{(}
          \AttributeTok{style =} \FunctionTok{cell\_fill}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ color),}
          \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ col, }\AttributeTok{rows =}\NormalTok{ i)}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{    \}}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Display table}
\NormalTok{gt\_table}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

You can also visualize the plot:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lpa.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{output\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"lpa"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"tidyLPA"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lpa}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_enum}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{model\_3\_class\_4.out)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\part{Including Auxiliary Variables}\label{part-including-auxiliary-variables}

\chapter{Manual 3-Step Covariate Only}\label{manual-3-step-covariate-only}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\textbf{Data source:}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  The first examples utilizes the public-use dataset, \emph{The Longitudinal Survey of American Youth} (\textbf{LSAY}): \href{https://www.lsay.org/}{See documentation here}
\item
  The second example utilizes a dataset on undergraduate \emph{Cheating} available from the \texttt{poLCA} package (Dayton, 1998): \href{https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/poLCA/poLCA.pdf}{See documentation here}
\end{enumerate}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load packages}\label{load-packages-6}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse) }\CommentTok{\#collection of R packages designed for data science}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here) }\CommentTok{\#helps with filepaths}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor) }\CommentTok{\#clean\_names}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt) }\CommentTok{\# create tables}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot) }\CommentTok{\# a ggplot theme}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(DiagrammeR) }\CommentTok{\# create path diagrams}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue) }\CommentTok{\# allows us to paste expressions into R code}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(data.table) }\CommentTok{\# used for \textasciigrave{}melt()\textasciigrave{} function  }
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(poLCA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(reshape2)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Our example is Mother's Education as a predictor of latent class membership of Math Attitudes

\begin{center}\includegraphics{13-one-covariate_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-2-1} \end{center}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Application: Longitudinal Study of American Youth, \textbf{Science Attitudes}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large \textbf{LCA Indicators \& Auxiliary Variables: Math Attitudes Example}} \\ 
{\small ~}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{\linewidth}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}ll}
\toprule
Name &  Variable Description \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
\multicolumn{2}{l}{} \\[2.5pt] 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
enjoy & I enjoy math. \\ 
useful & Math is useful in everyday problems. \\ 
logical & Math helps a person think logically. \\ 
job & It is important to know math to get a good job. \\ 
adult & I will use math in many ways as an adult. \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Auxiliary Variables} \\[2.5pt] 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
mothed & Level of education:  (1) less than high school, (2) high school diploma, (3) some college, (4) 4-year college, and (5) an advanced degree. \\ 
\bottomrule
\end{tabular*}
\end{table}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

The data can be found in the \texttt{data} folder and is called \texttt{lsay\_subset.csv}.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"lsay\_subset.csv"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}   \CommentTok{\# make variable names lowercase}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{female =} \FunctionTok{recode}\NormalTok{(gender, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{1}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{2}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\CommentTok{\# relabel values from 1,2 to 0,1}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(enjoy, useful, logical, job, adult),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"Variable"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}

    \AttributeTok{Count =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(value }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{Total =} \FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Proportion Endorsed}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(Count }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ Total, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Variable, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Proportion Endorsed}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{, Count)}

\FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(dframe) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**LCA Indicator Endorsement**"}\NormalTok{),}

\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{column\_labels.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{row\_group.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}

Mother's Education

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{mothed))}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}    1    2    3    4    5 NA\textquotesingle{}s }
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}  452 1732  304  368  158  102}
\end{Highlighting}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Manual ML Three-step}\label{manual-ml-three-step}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 1 - Class Enumeration w/ Auxiliary Specification}\label{step-1---class-enumeration-w-auxiliary-specification}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

This step is done after class enumeration (or after you have selected the best latent class model). In this example, the four class model was the best. Now, we re-estimate the four-class model using \texttt{optseed} for efficiency. The difference here is the \texttt{SAVEDATA} command, where I can save the posterior probabilities and the modal class assignment that will be used in steps two and three.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{step1  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step 1 {-} Three{-}Step using LSAL"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = enjoy useful logical job adult; }
\StringTok{   usevar = enjoy useful logical job adult;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   classes = c(4); }
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   auxiliary =  mothed  ! covariate "}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 0;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 568405;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File=savedata\_cov.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save=cprob;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"residual tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{    series = enjoy{-}adult(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data)}

\NormalTok{step1\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step1,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Step1.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"one\_cov.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lca.R"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{output\_lsay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"one\_cov.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lca}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_lsay)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\includegraphics[width=0.9\linewidth]{13-one-covariate_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-8-1}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 2 - Determine Measurement Error}\label{step-2---determine-measurement-error}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Extract logits for the classification probabilities for the most likely latent class

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{logit\_cprobs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"class\_counts"}\NormalTok{]]}
\NormalTok{                                       [[}\StringTok{"logitProbs.mostLikely"}\NormalTok{]])}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Extract saved dataset which is part of the mplusObject ``step1\_fit''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{savedata }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"savedata"}\NormalTok{]])}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Rename the column in savedata named ``C'' and change to ``N''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata)[}\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata) }\SpecialCharTok{\%in\%} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"MLCC"}\NormalTok{)] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"N"}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 3 - LCA Auxiliary Variable Model with 1 Covariate}\label{step-3---lca-auxiliary-variable-model-with-1-covariate}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{step3  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step3 {-} 3step LSAY"}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
 \StringTok{"nominal=N;}
\StringTok{  usevar = n;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  classes = c(4);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  usevar = mothed;"}\NormalTok{ ,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
 \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{  type = mixture; }
\StringTok{  starts = 0;"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{DEFINE =} 
   \StringTok{"center mothed (grandmean);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
  \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
 \StringTok{" \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{ }
\StringTok{  C on mothed;        ! covariate as predictor of C}

\StringTok{     \%C\#1\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[1,1]\}]; ! MUST EDIT if you do not have a 4{-}class model. }
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[1,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[1,3]\}];}

\StringTok{  \%C\#2\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[2,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[2,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[2,3]\}];}

\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  \%C\#3\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[3,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[3,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[3,3]\}];}


\StringTok{  \%C\#4\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[4,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[4,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[4,3]\}];"}\NormalTok{),}
  
 
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata), }
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ savedata)}

\NormalTok{step3\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step3,}
               \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Step3.dat"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"three\_cov.inp"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Covariates Relations}\label{covariates-relations}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

As of 1/1/2026, this code is outdated. See here: \url{https://github.com/michaelhallquist/MplusAutomation/issues/228}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{modelParams }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"three\_cov.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Extract information as data frame}
\NormalTok{cov }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(modelParams[[}\StringTok{"parameters"}\NormalTok{]][[}\StringTok{"unstandardized"}\NormalTok{]]) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
   \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(paramHeader, }\StringTok{"\^{}C\#}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.ON$"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\CommentTok{\#  mutate(param = str\_replace(param, "FEMALE", "Gender")) \%\textgreater{}\% \# Change this to your own covariates}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{str\_replace}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"MOTHED"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Mother\textquotesingle{}s Education"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{est =} \FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(est, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{),}
         \AttributeTok{se =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(se, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
         \AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(pval, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{latent\_class =} \FunctionTok{str\_replace}\NormalTok{(paramHeader, }\StringTok{"\^{}C\#(}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.ON$"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Class }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{1"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, est, se, pval, latent\_class) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{se =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"("}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(se,}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{unite}\NormalTok{(logit, est, se, }\AttributeTok{sep =} \StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, logit, pval, latent\_class) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\textless{}.001*"}\NormalTok{),}
                       \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{                              scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{)))) }

\NormalTok{or }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(modelParams[[}\StringTok{"parameters"}\NormalTok{]][[}\StringTok{"odds"}\NormalTok{]])}\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_detect}\NormalTok{(paramHeader, }\StringTok{"\^{}C\#}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.ON$"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
 \CommentTok{\# mutate(param = str\_replace(param, "FEMALE", "Gender")) \%\textgreater{}\% \# Change this to your own covariates}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{str\_replace}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"MOTHED"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Mother\textquotesingle{}s Education"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{est =} \FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(est, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{latent\_class =} \FunctionTok{str\_replace}\NormalTok{(paramHeader, }\StringTok{"\^{}C\#(}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{d+)}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.ON$"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Class }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{1"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CI =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"["}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(lower\_2}\FloatTok{.5}\NormalTok{ci, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{", "}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(upper\_2}\FloatTok{.5}\NormalTok{ci, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"]"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, est, CI, latent\_class) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{or =}\NormalTok{ est)}
  
\NormalTok{combined }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ or }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{full\_join}\NormalTok{(cov) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param, latent\_class, logit, pval, or, CI)}


\CommentTok{\# Create table}

\NormalTok{combined }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{groupname\_col =} \StringTok{"latent\_class"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{rowname\_col =} \StringTok{"param"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Covariate Results: Mother\textquotesingle{}s Education on Class"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{logit =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Logit (*se*)"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{or =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Odds Ratio"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{CI =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"95\% CI"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*p*{-}value"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{sub\_missing}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
              \AttributeTok{missing\_text =} \StringTok{""}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{sub\_values}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{values =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"999.000"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{replacement =} \StringTok{"{-}"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{opt\_align\_table\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  gt}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.font.names =} \StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}   
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Reference Class: 4"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{cells\_title}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{groups =} \StringTok{"title"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Automated Three-Step}\label{automated-three-step}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\emph{Application: Undergraduate Cheating behavior}

``Dichotomous self-report responses by 319 undergraduates to four questions about cheating behavior'' (poLCA, 2016).

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Prepare data

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\FunctionTok{data}\NormalTok{(cheating)}

\NormalTok{cheating }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ cheating }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }

\NormalTok{df\_cheat }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{  cheating }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                                  
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                                  
  \FunctionTok{mutate\_all}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{funs}\NormalTok{(.}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                                
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{gpa =}\NormalTok{ cheating}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{gpa)}

\CommentTok{\# Detaching packages that mask the dpylr functions }
\FunctionTok{detach}\NormalTok{(package}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{poLCA, }\AttributeTok{unload =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{detach}\NormalTok{(package}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{MASS, }\AttributeTok{unload =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{R3STEP}\label{r3step}

\textbf{R3STEP} incorporates latent class predictors with mixture models. However, it is recommended to use the manual three-step.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{center}\includegraphics{13-one-covariate_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-15-1} \end{center}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Run the \textbf{R3STEP} model with \texttt{gpa} as the latent class predictor}{Run the R3STEP model with gpa as the latent class predictor}}\label{run-the-r3step-model-with-gpa-as-the-latent-class-predictor}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{m\_stepr  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"R3STEP {-} GPA as Predictor"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
   \StringTok{"categorical = lieexam{-}copyexam; }
\StringTok{    usevar = lieexam{-}copyexam;}
\StringTok{    auxiliary = gpa (R3STEP);}
\StringTok{    classes = c(2);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 500 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat patterns tech11 tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{     series = lieexam{-}copyexam(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(df\_cheat),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ df\_cheat)}

\NormalTok{m\_stepr\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(m\_stepr, }
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"auto\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"r3step.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"auto\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c2\_r3step.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Regression slopes and odds ratios}\label{regression-slopes-and-odds-ratios}

\begin{verbatim}
TESTS OF CATEGORICAL LATENT VARIABLE MULTINOMIAL LOGISTIC REGRESSIONS USING
THE 3-STEP PROCEDURE

   WARNING:  LISTWISE DELETION IS APPLIED TO THE AUXILIARY VARIABLES IN THE
   ANALYSIS.  TO AVOID LISTWISE DELETION, DATA IMPUTATION CAN BE USED
   FOR THE AUXILIARY VARIABLES FOLLOWED BY ANALYSIS WITH TYPE=IMPUTATION.
   NUMBER OF DELETED OBSERVATIONS:  4
   NUMBER OF OBSERVATIONS USED:  315

                                                    Two-Tailed
                    Estimate       S.E.  Est./S.E.    P-Value

 C#1        ON
    GPA               -0.698      0.255     -2.739      0.006

 Intercepts
    C#1               -0.241      0.460     -0.523      0.601

Parameterization using Reference Class 1

 C#2        ON
    GPA                0.698      0.255      2.739      0.006

 Intercepts
    C#2                0.241      0.460      0.523      0.601


ODDS RATIOS FOR TESTS OF CATEGORICAL LATENT VARIABLE MULTINOMIAL LOGISTIC REGRESSIONS
USING THE 3-STEP PROCEDURE

                                                95% C.I.
                    Estimate       S.E. Lower 2.5% Upper 2.5%

 C#1        ON
    GPA                0.498      0.127      0.302      0.820


Parameterization using Reference Class 1

 C#2        ON
    GPA                2.009      0.512      1.220      3.310
\end{verbatim}

\chapter{Manual 3-Step Distal Only}\label{manual-3-step-distal-only}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\textbf{Data source:}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  The first examples utilizes the public-use dataset, \emph{The Longitudinal Survey of American Youth} (\textbf{LSAY}): \href{https://www.lsay.org/}{See documentation here}
\item
  The second example utilizes a dataset on undergraduate \emph{Cheating} available from the \texttt{poLCA} package (Dayton, 1998): \href{https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/poLCA/poLCA.pdf}{See documentation here}
\end{enumerate}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load packages}\label{load-packages-7}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse) }\CommentTok{\#collection of R packages designed for data science}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here) }\CommentTok{\#helps with filepaths}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor) }\CommentTok{\#clean\_names}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt) }\CommentTok{\# create tables}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot) }\CommentTok{\# a ggplot theme}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(DiagrammeR) }\CommentTok{\# create path diagrams}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue) }\CommentTok{\# allows us to paste expressions into R code}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(data.table) }\CommentTok{\# used for \textasciigrave{}melt()\textasciigrave{} function  }
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(poLCA)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(reshape2)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Our example is Math IRT Score as a distal outcome of Math Attitude classes

\begin{center}\includegraphics{14-one-distal_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-2-1} \end{center}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Application: Longitudinal Study of American Youth, \textbf{Math Attitudes}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large \textbf{LCA Indicators \& Auxiliary Variables: Math Attitudes Example}} \\ 
{\small ~}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{\linewidth}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}ll}
\toprule
Name &  Variable Description \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
\multicolumn{2}{l}{} \\[2.5pt]

The data can be found in the \texttt{data} folder and is called \texttt{lsay\_subset.csv}.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"lsay\_subset.csv"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{clean\_names}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}   \CommentTok{\# make variable names lowercase}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{female =} \FunctionTok{recode}\NormalTok{(gender, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{1}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{2}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\CommentTok{\# relabel values from 1,2 to 0,1}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

    \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(enjoy, useful, logical, job, adult),}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"Variable"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}

  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Count =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(value }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{Total =} \FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{.groups =} \StringTok{"drop"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Proportion Endorsed}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(Count }\SpecialCharTok{/}\NormalTok{ Total, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Variable, }\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Proportion Endorsed}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\NormalTok{, Count)}

\FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(dframe) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**LCA Indicator Endorsement**"}\NormalTok{),}

\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{column\_labels.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{row\_group.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}

Math IRT Score

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{summary}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{math\_irt)}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA\textquotesingle{}s }
