Alternative plot:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{jo\_output }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"joint\_occurrence"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"jo\_model"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three.out"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{jo\_prob }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(jo\_output}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{transitionProbs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{probability)}

\NormalTok{c1\_labels }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pro{-}Math }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(46\%)"}\NormalTok{, }
               \StringTok{"Math Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(18\%)"}\NormalTok{, }
               \StringTok{"Math Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(19\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Anti{-}Math }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(17\%)"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{c2\_labels }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pro{-}Science }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(30\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Science Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(26\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Science Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(8\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Anti{-}Science }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(36\%)"}\NormalTok{)}


\CommentTok{\# T1 → T2}
\NormalTok{c1\_c2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{expand.grid}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{C1 =}\NormalTok{ c1\_labels, }\AttributeTok{C2 =}\NormalTok{ c2\_labels) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{P12 =}\NormalTok{ jo\_prob[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(jo\_prob), }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{]) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{P12 =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(P12, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{))}


\CommentTok{\# Plot for T1 {-}\textgreater{} T2}
 \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(c1\_c2, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{axis1 =}\NormalTok{ C1, }\AttributeTok{axis2 =}\NormalTok{ C2, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ P12)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_alluvium}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{fill =}\NormalTok{ C1), }\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{alpha =} \FloatTok{0.7}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Make the stratum rectangles white instead of gray:}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_stratum}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_text}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{stat =} \StringTok{"stratum"}\NormalTok{, }
    \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{after\_stat}\NormalTok{(stratum)), }
    \AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{3.5}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \CommentTok{\# Label the flows themselves with the probability}
 \CommentTok{\# geom\_text(aes(label = P12),}
\CommentTok{\#            stat = "flow", nudge\_x = .2, size = 5) +}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_discrete}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{limits =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Math Attitudes"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Science Attitudes"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{expand =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(.}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, .}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{subtitle =} \StringTok{"Math and Science Attitudes"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Joint Occurrence Model"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{""}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_minimal}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{family =} \StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"none"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{axis.text.x =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"black"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{axis.title.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),  }
    \AttributeTok{axis.text.y  =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(), }
    \AttributeTok{axis.ticks.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.major =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(), }
    \AttributeTok{panel.grid.minor =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),  }
    \AttributeTok{plot.subtitle =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{face =} \StringTok{"italic"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{plot.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract Probabilities}
\NormalTok{jo\_prob\_matrix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.matrix}\NormalTok{(jo\_output}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{transitionProbs}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{probability)}


\CommentTok{\# Label Classes}
\NormalTok{c1\_labels }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pro{-}Math }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(46\%)"}\NormalTok{, }
               \StringTok{"Math Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(18\%)"}\NormalTok{, }
               \StringTok{"Math Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(19\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Anti{-}Math }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(17\%)"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{c2\_labels }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Pro{-}Science }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(30\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Science Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(26\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Science Ambivalent }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Elevated Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(8\%)"}\NormalTok{,}
               \StringTok{"Anti{-}Science }\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{with Minimal Utility Value}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{(36\%)"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Number of Classes for each LCA}
\NormalTok{C1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(c1\_labels)  }
\NormalTok{C2 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(c2\_labels)  }

\CommentTok{\# Format Probability Table}
\NormalTok{jo\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{matrix}\NormalTok{(jo\_prob\_matrix, }\AttributeTok{nrow =}\NormalTok{ C1, }\AttributeTok{ncol =}\NormalTok{ C2, }\AttributeTok{byrow =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\FunctionTok{rownames}\NormalTok{(jo\_df) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ c1\_labels}
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(jo\_df) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ c2\_labels}
\NormalTok{t\_matrix }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(jo\_df) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rownames\_to\_column}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{var =} \StringTok{"Math Attitudes"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Create Probability Table}
\NormalTok{t\_matrix }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rowname\_col =} \StringTok{"Math Attitudes"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_stubhead}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Math Attitudes"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Joint Distribution Matrix**"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{subtitle =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Distribution Math Attitude Classes (Rows) conditioned on Science Attitude Classes (Columns)**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{2}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{,}\AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_spanner}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Science Attitudes"}\NormalTok{,}\AttributeTok{columns =} \DecValTok{2}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{(C2}\SpecialCharTok{+}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))}\CommentTok{\#\%\textgreater{}\% }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\chapter{Growth Mixture Models}\label{growth-mixture-models}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\emph{Example: Longitudinal Study of American Youth}

\textbf{Data source:} : This example looks at science IRT scores over time (Grades 7-12). \href{https://www.lsay.org/}{See documentation here}. Covariates include gender and interest in science issues in 7th grade.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load Packages}\label{load-packages-13}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(DiagrammeR)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(Hmisc)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Path Diagram}\label{path-diagram-1}

\begin{center}\includegraphics{25-gmm_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-2-1} \end{center}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Read in LSAY dataset

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lsay\_sci }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"lsay\_sci\_gmm.csv"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{id =}\NormalTok{ CASENUM,}
    \AttributeTok{female =}\NormalTok{ GENDER,}
    \AttributeTok{interest7 =}\NormalTok{ AB34D,}
    \AttributeTok{sci7 =}\NormalTok{ ASCIIRT,}
    \AttributeTok{sci8 =}\NormalTok{ CSCIIRT,}
    \AttributeTok{sci9 =}\NormalTok{ ESCIIRT, }
    \AttributeTok{sci10 =}\NormalTok{ GSCIIRT,}
    \AttributeTok{sci11 =}\NormalTok{ ISCIIRT,}
    \AttributeTok{sci12 =}\NormalTok{ KSCIIRT}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{female =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(female }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Descriptive Statistics}\label{descriptive-statistics-13}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lsay\_sci }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{id) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  psych}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{describe}\NormalTok{()}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}           vars    n  mean    sd median trimmed   mad   min}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} female       1 5945  0.49  0.50   0.00    0.49  0.00  0.00}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} interest7    2 2992  1.80  0.76   2.00    1.76  1.48  1.00}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci7         3 3071 50.41 10.20  50.04   50.18 11.33 26.14}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci8         4 2527 54.05 11.16  54.64   54.25 12.11 22.82}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci9         5 2326 58.69 11.24  60.40   59.15 11.05 27.36}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci10        6 4690 60.32 11.02  60.84   60.50 11.72 26.97}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci11        7 3592 64.10 11.21  64.75   64.51 11.10 24.44}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci12        8 2826 65.85 11.65  66.25   66.28 11.14 26.38}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{}             max range  skew kurtosis   se}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} female     1.00  1.00  0.04    {-}2.00 0.01}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} interest7  3.00  2.00  0.34    {-}1.20 0.01}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci7      88.03 61.89  0.20    {-}0.46 0.18}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} sci8      83.94 61.12 {-}0.17    {-}0.65 0.22}

\subsubsection{Spaghetti Plot}\label{spaghetti-plot}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{plot\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ lsay\_sci[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{500}\NormalTok{,] }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{drop\_na}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{starts\_with}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sci"}\NormalTok{),  }
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"grade"}\NormalTok{,          }
               \AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{grade =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(grade, }
                        \AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"sci7"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sci8"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sci9"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sci10"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sci11"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"sci12"}\NormalTok{), }
                        \AttributeTok{labels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{9}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{,}\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{)))}

\NormalTok{mean\_sci }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ plot\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{drop\_na}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(grade) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{mean\_response =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(value),}
            \AttributeTok{se\_response =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(value) }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sqrt}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{n}\NormalTok{()))}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}                                                                   
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ plot\_data, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ grade, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ id), }\AttributeTok{alpha =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}     
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ plot\_data, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ grade, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ value, }\AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ id), }\AttributeTok{alpha =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}      
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data=}\NormalTok{mean\_sci, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x=}\NormalTok{grade, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ mean\_response), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"Blue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}               
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data=}\NormalTok{mean\_sci, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x=}\NormalTok{grade, }\AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ mean\_response, }\AttributeTok{group =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"blue"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{geom\_errorbar}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{data =}\NormalTok{ mean\_sci, }\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ grade, }\AttributeTok{ymin =}\NormalTok{ mean\_response }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ se\_response, }
                                      \AttributeTok{ymax =}\NormalTok{ mean\_response }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ se\_response),}
                \AttributeTok{width =} \FloatTok{0.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{1.2}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{color =} \StringTok{"blue"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}  
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Spaghetti Plot with Mean Line and Error Bars"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x=}\StringTok{"Grade"}\NormalTok{, }
       \AttributeTok{y=}\StringTok{"Science Score"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}                                                           
  \FunctionTok{theme\_cowplot}\NormalTok{()                                                                              }

\subsection{Unconditional Growth Mixture Model}\label{unconditional-growth-mixture-model}

This MplusAutomation code loops through the class-specific statements to include freeing variances and covariances.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{gmm\_6 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k)\{ }
  
  \CommentTok{\# This MODEL section changes the model specification}
\NormalTok{  MODEL }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{k, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(i) \{}
    \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}
\StringTok{    \%c\#\{i\}\%}
\StringTok{    s WITH I;      ! covariances are freely estimated}
\StringTok{    sci7{-}sci12;    ! variances are freely estimated}
\StringTok{    "}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  \}), }\AttributeTok{collapse =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
  
\NormalTok{  gmm\_enum  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"GMM \{k\}{-}Class"}\NormalTok{), }
    
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"usevar = sci7{-}sci12; }
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\}); "}\NormalTok{),}
    
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 200 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 12;"}\NormalTok{,}
    
    \AttributeTok{MODEL =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{       i s | sci7@0 sci8@1 sci9@2 sci10@3 sci11@4 sci12@5;}
\StringTok{       }
\StringTok{       \{MODEL\}"}\NormalTok{), }\CommentTok{\# The \textasciigrave{}MODEL\textasciigrave{} object is placed here.}
    
    \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"tech1 tech11 tech14 sampstat standardized svalues;"}\NormalTok{,}
    
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
    \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FILE IS savedata\_c\{k\}.dat;}
\StringTok{     SAVE = cprobabilities;"}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} \StringTok{"type=plot3;}
\StringTok{          series = sci7{-}sci12(*)"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_sci),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_sci)}

\NormalTok{gmm\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(gmm\_enum, }
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_lsay.dat"}\NormalTok{)),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_gmm\_lsay.inp"}\NormalTok{)) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Table of Fit}\label{table-of-fit-4}

First, extract data:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{output\_gmm }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"gmm\_enum"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract fit indices}
\NormalTok{enum\_extract }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{LatexSummaryTable}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  output\_gmm,}
  \AttributeTok{keepCols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"Title"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"Parameters"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"LL"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"BLRT\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"T11\_VLMR\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"Observations"}
\NormalTok{  ),}
  \AttributeTok{sortBy =} \StringTok{"Title"}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract lowest class size}
\NormalTok{min\_sizes }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_df}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(output\_gmm), }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  model }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_gmm[[.x]]}
\NormalTok{  min\_size }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(model}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{class\_counts}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{modelEstimated}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{proportion) }\SpecialCharTok{*} \DecValTok{100}
  \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model =}\NormalTok{ .x, }\AttributeTok{min\_cs =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(min\_size, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{\})}

\CommentTok{\# Combine dataframe}
\NormalTok{combined }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{cbind}\NormalTok{(enum\_extract, min\_sizes)}

\CommentTok{\# Calculate additional fit indices}
\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ combined }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CAIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{AWE =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.}\DecValTok{5} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ BIC) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{expSIC =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{BF =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cmPk =}\NormalTok{ expSIC }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(expSIC)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Title, Parameters, min\_cs, LL, BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE, BLRT\_PValue, T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BF, cmPk) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(Parameters)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Then, create table:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_table1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Model Fit Summary Table**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Title =} \StringTok{"Classes"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Parameters =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Par"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{min\_cs =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Min. Class Size"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{LL =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*LL*"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{T11\_VLMR\_PValue =} \StringTok{"VLMR"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{BLRT\_PValue =} \StringTok{"BLRT"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{BF =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"BF"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{cmPk =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*cmPk*"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"*Note.* Par = Parameters; *LL* = model log likelihood;}
\StringTok{BIC = Bayesian information criterion;}
\StringTok{aBIC = sample size adjusted BIC; CAIC = consistent Akaike information criterion;}
\StringTok{AWE = approximate weight of evidence criterion;}
\StringTok{BLRT = bootstrapped likelihood ratio test p{-}value;}
\StringTok{VLMR = Vuong{-}Lo{-}Mendell{-}Rubin adjusted likelihood ratio test p{-}value;}
\StringTok{*cmPk* = approximate correct model probability."}
\NormalTok{    ),}
\AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{cells\_title}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{column\_labels.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{3}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{),}
             \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_missing}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{,}
              \AttributeTok{missing\_text =} \StringTok{"{-}{-}"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{9}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{fns =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x)}
      \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textless{}.001"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{             scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{01}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}
    \DecValTok{11}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{fns =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{ (x)}
      \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textgreater{}100"}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{             scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{01}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}  
  \FunctionTok{tab\_style}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{style =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \FunctionTok{cell\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ),}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ BIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(BIC[}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{)]) }\CommentTok{\# Change this to the number of classes you estimated}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ aBIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ aBIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(aBIC[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ CAIC,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ CAIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(CAIC[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ AWE,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ AWE }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(AWE[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ cmPk,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{  cmPk }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(cmPk[}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{])}
\NormalTok{     ),    }
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BF,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{  BF }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{( }
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{  T11\_VLMR\_PValue,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}  \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, T11\_VLMR\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{)),}
    \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}
     \AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{  BLRT\_PValue,}
     \AttributeTok{row =}  \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(BLRT\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(BLRT\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, BLRT\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\textsuperscript{\textit{1}}\emph{Note.} Par = Parameters; \emph{LL} = model log likelihood;
BIC = Bayesian information criterion;
aBIC = sample size adjusted BIC; CAIC = consistent Akaike information criterion;
AWE = approximate weight of evidence criterion;
BLRT = bootstrapped likelihood ratio test p-value;
VLMR = Vuong-Lo-Mendell-Rubin adjusted likelihood ratio test p-value;
\emph{cmPk} = approximate correct model probability.\\

\subsection{Covariates Growth Mixture Model}\label{covariates-growth-mixture-model}

Two covariates were used in the GMM analysis and were related to the latent class variable: gender and interest in science issues in 7th grade.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{step1  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"GMM with Covariates"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"usevar = sci7{-}sci12}
\StringTok{  female interest7;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   classes = c(4);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 200 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 12;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =} 
    \StringTok{"\%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    i s on female interest7;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    i s | sci7@0 sci8@1 sci9@2 sci10@3 sci11@4 sci12@5;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    \%c\#1\%}
\StringTok{    s WITH I;      ! covariances are freely estimated}
\StringTok{    sci7{-}sci12;    ! variances are freely estimated}
\StringTok{    i s on female interest7;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    \%c\#2\%}
\StringTok{    s WITH I;     }
\StringTok{    sci7{-}sci12;   }
\StringTok{    i s on female interest7;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    \%c\#3\%}
\StringTok{    s WITH I;     }
\StringTok{    sci7{-}sci12;    }
\StringTok{    i s on female interest7;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{    \%c\#4\%}
\StringTok{    s WITH I;     }
\StringTok{    sci7{-}sci12;   }
\StringTok{    i s on female interest7;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"tech1 tech11 tech14 sampstat standardized svalues;"}\NormalTok{,}
    
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
    \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"FILE IS savedata\_c4.dat;}
\StringTok{     SAVE = cprobabilities;"}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} \StringTok{"type=plot3;}
\StringTok{          series = sci7{-}sci12(*)"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_sci),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_sci)}

\NormalTok{step1\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step1,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsubsection{Plot GMM}\label{plot-gmm-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{gmm\_cov }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"gmm"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"gmm\_cov.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{plotGrowthMixtures}\NormalTok{(gmm\_cov, }\AttributeTok{estimated =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{rawdata =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }
                   \AttributeTok{time\_scale =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{4}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{alpha\_range =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{, }\FloatTok{0.01}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{bw =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\part{Workflow for Published Manuscripts}\label{part-workflow-for-published-manuscripts}

\chapter{Distal Outcomes (Nylund-Gibson, Grimm, \& Masyn, 2019)}\label{distal-outcomes-nylund-gibson-grimm-masyn-2019}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

This chapter replicates the workflow in Nylund-Gibson et al.~(2019).

Citation: \href{https://doi.org/10.1177/01987429211067214}{Nylund-Gibson, K., Grimm, R. P., \& Masyn, K. E. (2019). Prediction from latent classes: A demonstration of different approaches to include distal outcomes in mixture models. \emph{Structural equation modeling: A multidisciplinary Journal}, \emph{26}(6), 967-985.}

\section{Data overview}\label{data-overview}

\textbf{Math attitude indicator:}

Five math attitudinal variables were used as the response indicators for the LCA model.

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \emph{enjoym} - I enjoy math.
\item
  \emph{goodm} - I am good at math.
\item
  \emph{udnerstandm} - I usually understand what we are doing in math.
\item
  nervousm - Doing math often makes me nervous or upset.
\item
  scaredm - I often get scared when I open my math book and see a page of problems.
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Covariate:}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  female - gender (female = 1; male = 0)
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Distal outcomes:}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  mathjob - Math job (1 = student felt that math was useful for a later job; 0 = student did not feel that math was useful for a later job)
\item
  mathirt - Math IRT score in grade 9 (continuous, higher math scores represent higher math achievement)
\end{itemize}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Enumeration - Maysn (2017)}\label{enumeration---maysn-2017}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lca\_math  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_math\_enum  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
      
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class"}\NormalTok{), }
  
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"categorical = enjoym{-}scaredm; }
\StringTok{     usevar = enjoym{-}scaredm;}
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\}); "}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 200 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat residual tech11 tech14 svalues;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{    series = enjoym{-}scaredm(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_df),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_df)}

\NormalTok{lca\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(lca\_math\_enum, }
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_enum.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Distal-as-indicator approach}\label{distal-as-indicator-approach}

The distal-as-indicator approach, also called the 1-step approach because the latent class variable and the distal outcome means are measured in one modeling step (Vermunt, 2010), the distal outcome is part of the measurement model of the latent variable; that is, the distal outcome is treated as an indicator of the latent class variable (Muthén \& Shedden, 1999).

For this model, five math attitude variables, the gender covariate, and the two distal outcomes are included as class indicators.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{dasi }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_enum  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
      
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class"}\NormalTok{), }
  
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"categorical = enjoym{-}mathjob female; }
\StringTok{     usevar = enjoym{-}female;}
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\});"}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 200 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat residual tech1 tech8 tech10 tech11 tech14 svalues;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_df),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_df)}

\NormalTok{dasi\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(lca\_enum, }
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum\_dasi"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"dasi.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum\_dasi"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_dasi.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Table of fit}\label{table-of-fit-5}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"extract\_mplus\_info.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Define the directory where all of the .out files are located.}
\NormalTok{output\_dir }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_dasi"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Get all .out files}
\NormalTok{output\_files }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Process all .out files into one dataframe}
\NormalTok{final\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract Sample\_Size from final\_data}
\NormalTok{sample\_size }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Sample\_Size)}

\CommentTok{\# Read in .out files into \textasciigrave{}MplusAutomation\textasciigrave{}}
\NormalTok{output\_dasi }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum\_dasi"}\NormalTok{),}
                          \AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"dasi"}\NormalTok{,}
                          \AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract fit indices}
\NormalTok{enum\_extract }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{LatexSummaryTable}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  output\_dasi,}
  \AttributeTok{keepCols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"Title"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"Parameters"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"LL"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"BLRT\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"T11\_VLMR\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"Observations"}
\NormalTok{  ),}
  \AttributeTok{sortBy =} \StringTok{"Title"}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Calculate additional fit indices}
\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ enum\_extract }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CAIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{AWE =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.}\DecValTok{5} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ BIC) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{expSIC =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{BF =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cmPk =}\NormalTok{ expSIC }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(expSIC)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{9}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{13}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{14}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(Parameters)}

\CommentTok{\# Merge columns with LL replications and class size from \textasciigrave{}final\_data\textasciigrave{}}
\NormalTok{merged\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Title =} \FunctionTok{str\_trim}\NormalTok{(Title)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    final\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{      dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{        Class\_Model,}
\NormalTok{        Perc\_Convergence,}
\NormalTok{        Replicated\_LL\_Perc,}
\NormalTok{        Smallest\_Class,}
\NormalTok{        Smallest\_Class\_Perc}
\NormalTok{      ),}
    \AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Title"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Class\_Model"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Smallest\_Class =} \FunctionTok{coalesce}\NormalTok{(Smallest\_Class, final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class[}\FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(Title, final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Class\_Model)])) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{relocate}\NormalTok{(Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc, }\AttributeTok{.after =}\NormalTok{ LL) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Smallest\_Class\_Combined =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(Smallest\_Class, }\StringTok{"\textbackslash{}u00A0("}\NormalTok{, Smallest\_Class\_Perc, }\StringTok{"\%)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class\_Perc) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    Title,}
\NormalTok{    Parameters,}
\NormalTok{    LL,}
\NormalTok{    Perc\_Convergence,}
\NormalTok{    Replicated\_LL\_Perc,}
\NormalTok{    BIC,}
\NormalTok{    aBIC,}
\NormalTok{    CAIC,}
\NormalTok{    AWE,}
\NormalTok{    T11\_VLMR\_PValue,}
\NormalTok{    BLRT\_PValue,}
\NormalTok{    Smallest\_Class\_Combined,}
\NormalTok{    BF,}
\NormalTok{    cmPk}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}

  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{9}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BLRT\_PValue),}

\NormalTok{        x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textless{}.001"}\NormalTok{, scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{01}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ))}

            \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{          T11\_VLMR\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{,}
          \ConstantTok{NA}
\NormalTok{        )}
\NormalTok{      ),}

                       \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(BLRT\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, BLRT\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{fit\_table1}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

The results from the distal-as-indicator approach suggested a 4-class model as well. Though there were some differences in the emergent latent classes compared to the classes from the unconditional LCA model without distals, the substantive interpretation would largely remain the same with respect to the math attitudinal variables.

The distal mean of the binary outcome variable (in this case, \emph{math job}) can be located in the ``RESULTS IN PROBABILITY SCALE'' section. The excerpt below presents the distal mean of \emph{math job} for Latent Class 1.

\includegraphics[width=4.6875in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_1.png}

The distal mean of the continuous outcome variable (in this case, \emph{math IRT score}) can be located in the ``MODEL RESULTS'' section. The excerpt below shows the distal mean of the \emph{math IRT score} for Latent Class 1.

\includegraphics[width=4.6875in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_2.png}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lca}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_dasi}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c4\_dasi.out)}
\end{Highlighting}

\section{Adding constraints to test the distal means}\label{adding-constraints-to-test-the-distal-means}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{dasi\_cons  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"D as I with constraints"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = enjoym{-}mathjob female;}
\StringTok{   usevar = enjoym{-}female;}
\StringTok{   classes = c(4);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
  \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 200 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
    \StringTok{" \%overall\%}

\StringTok{      \%c\#1\%}
\StringTok{      [mathjob$1] (dj1);}
\StringTok{      [mathirt]   (dm1);}
\StringTok{      [female$1]  (df1);}

\StringTok{      \%c\#2\%}
\StringTok{      [mathjob$1] (dj2);}
\StringTok{      [mathirt]   (dm2);}
\StringTok{      [female$1]  (df2);}

\StringTok{      \%c\#3\%}
\StringTok{      [mathjob$1] (dj3);}
\StringTok{      [mathirt]   (dm3);}
\StringTok{      [female$1]  (df3);}

\StringTok{      \%c\#4\%}
\StringTok{      [mathjob$1] (dj4);}
\StringTok{      [mathirt]   (dm4);}
\StringTok{      [female$1]  (df4);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File=3step\_savedata.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save=cprob;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"residual tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELCONSTRAINT =} 
  \StringTok{"New (dj1v2 dj1v3 dj1v4 dj2v3 dj2v4 dj3v4}
\StringTok{       dm1v2 dm1v3 dm1v4 dm2v3 dm2v4 dm3v4}
\StringTok{       df1v2 df1v3 df1v4 df2v3 df2v4 df3v4);}

\StringTok{    dj1v2 = dj1{-}dj2;}
\StringTok{    dj1v3 = dj1{-}dj3;}
\StringTok{    dj1v4 = dj1{-}dj4;}
\StringTok{    dj2v3 = dj2{-}dj3;}
\StringTok{    dj2v4 = dj2{-}dj4;}
\StringTok{    dj3v4 = dj3{-}dj4;}

\StringTok{    dm1v2 = dm1{-}dm2;}
\StringTok{    dm1v3 = dm1{-}dm3;}
\StringTok{    dm1v4 = dm1{-}dm4;}
\StringTok{    dm2v3 = dm2{-}dm3;}
\StringTok{    dm2v4 = dm2{-}dm4;}
\StringTok{    dm3v4 = dm3{-}dm4;}

\StringTok{    df1v2 = df1{-}df2;}
\StringTok{    df1v3 = df1{-}df3;}
\StringTok{    df1v4 = df1{-}df4;}
\StringTok{    df2v3 = df2{-}df3;}
\StringTok{    df2v4 = df2{-}df4;}
\StringTok{    df3v4 = df3{-}df4;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_df),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_df)}

\NormalTok{dasi\_cons\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(dasi\_cons,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"constraints"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"dasi\_constraints.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"constraints"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"dasi\_constraints.inp"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The significance of the newly specified parameters (e.g., DJ1V2, DM1V2) indicates whether there are statistically significant differences between the latent classes. For example, a significant p-value for DM1V2 suggests a meaningful difference in the distal outcome of the math IRT score between Latent Class 1 and Latent Class 2.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Manual 3-step}\label{manual-3-step}

\subsection{Step 1 Class Enumeration w/ Auxiliary Specification}\label{step-1-class-enumeration-w-auxiliary-specification}

The first step involves identifying the best-fitting unconditional model and saving the posterior probabilities and modal class assignment for that model (i.e., savedata: save=cprob;) and specifying any distal outcome variables as auxiliary variables so that they are included in the new data file saved in the savedata command.

This step is done after class enumeration. In this example, the four class model was the best. Therefore, we are re-estimating the four-class model using \texttt{optseed} for efficiency. The \texttt{optseed} can be found in the ``RANDOM STARTS RESULTS RANKED FROM THE BEST TO THE WORST LOGLIKELIHOOD VALUES'' section. Any random start value that yields the best log-likelihood can be used as the \texttt{optseed}, and they should produce identical parameter estimates.

\includegraphics[width=5.48958in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_3.png}

In the \texttt{SAVEDATA} command, we can save the posterior probabilities and the modal class assignment for steps two and three.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ml\_step1  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step 1 "}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = enjoym{-}scaredm; }
\StringTok{   usevar = enjoym{-}scaredm;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   classes = c(4); }
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   auxiliary =   ! list all potential covariates and distals here}
\StringTok{   female        ! covariate}
\StringTok{   mathjob mathirt;      ! distal math test score in 9th grade "}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 0;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 484501;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File = 3step\_savedata.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save = cprob;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"residual tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_df),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_df)}

\NormalTok{ml\_step1\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(ml\_step1,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ML\_step1.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ML\_step1.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 2 - Determine Measurement Error}\label{step-2---determine-measurement-error-6}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{output\_lsay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ML\_step1.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{logit\_cprobs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"class\_counts"}\NormalTok{]]}
\NormalTok{                                       [[}\StringTok{"logitProbs.mostLikely"}\NormalTok{]])}

\NormalTok{savedata\_lsay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"savedata"}\NormalTok{]])}

\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_lsay)[}\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_lsay)}\SpecialCharTok{==}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"N"}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 3 - Add Auxiliary Variables}\label{step-3---add-auxiliary-variables-3}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{ML\_step3  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step3 "}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
 \StringTok{"nominal = N;}
\StringTok{  usevar = n;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  classes = c(4);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  usevar = female mathjob mathirt;"}\NormalTok{ ,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
 \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{  type = mixture; }
\StringTok{  starts = 100 50;}
\StringTok{  processors = 4;"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{DEFINE =} 
   \StringTok{"center female (grandmean);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
  \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
 \StringTok{" \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{ }
\StringTok{  mathirt on female; ! covariate as a predictor of the distal outcome}
\StringTok{  mathjob on female;}
\StringTok{  C on female;        ! covariate as predictor of C}

\StringTok{     \%C\#1\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[1,1]\}]; ! MUST EDIT if you do not have a 4{-}class model. }
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[1,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[1,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m1);    ! conditional distal mean }
\StringTok{  mathirt;          ! conditional distal variance (freely estimated)}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j1);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}

\StringTok{  \%C\#2\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[2,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[2,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[2,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m2);}
\StringTok{  mathirt;}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j2);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  \%C\#3\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[3,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[3,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[3,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m3);}
\StringTok{  mathirt;}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j3);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}

\StringTok{  \%C\#4\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[4,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[4,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[4,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m4);}
\StringTok{  mathirt; }
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j4);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;  "}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELCONSTRAINT =} 
   \StringTok{"New (dm1v2 dm1v3 dm2v3 dm1v4 dm2v4 dm3v4 }
\StringTok{         dj1v2 dj1v3 dj2v3 dj1v4 dj2v4 dj3v4 }
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{    dm1v2 = m1{-}m2;  ! test pairwise distal mean differences of IRT score}
\StringTok{    dm1v3 = m1{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    dm2v3 = m2{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    dm1v4 = m1{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dm2v4 = m2{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dm3v4 = m3{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dj1v2 = j1{-}j2;  ! test pairwise distal mean differences of math job}
\StringTok{    dj1v3 = j1{-}j3;}
\StringTok{    dj2v3 = j2{-}j3;}
\StringTok{    dj1v4 = j1{-}j4;}
\StringTok{    dj2v4 = j2{-}j4;}
\StringTok{    dj3v4 = j3{-}j4;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELTEST =} \StringTok{"     ! omnibus test of distal means }
\StringTok{    m1=m2;}
\StringTok{    m2=m3;}
\StringTok{    m3=m4;}
\StringTok{    j1=j2;}
\StringTok{    j2=j3;}
\StringTok{    j3=j4;"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_lsay), }
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ savedata\_lsay)}

\NormalTok{step3\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(ML\_step3,}
               \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ML\_step3.dat"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"ML\_step3.inp"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The distal means of the outcome variables are located in the `MODEL RESULTS' section. The excerpt below presents the distal means of \emph{math job} and \emph{math IRT score} for Latent Class 1.

\includegraphics[width=4.6875in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_4.png}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{BCH approach}\label{bch-approach}

The BCH method is very similar to the three-step approach except that instead of calculating the average classification error in the second step, classification errors for each individual are computed, and the inverse logits of those individual-level error rates are used as weights in the third step rather than using the modal class assignment as imperfect latent class indicator. Advantages with this approach are that it appears to be more resistant to shifts in the latent classes in the third step (which is a problem in the manual 3-step in Mplus) and can often be used irrespective of variances being equal or unequal across latent classes (which can be a problem with the LTB approach).

\subsection{Step 1 - Class Enumeration w/ Auxiliary Specification and BCH Weights}\label{step-1---class-enumeration-w-auxiliary-specification-and-bch-weights}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{step1\_bch  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step 1 {-} BCH Method"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = enjoym{-}scaredm; }
\StringTok{   usevar = enjoym{-}scaredm;}
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   classes = c(4); }
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   auxiliary =   ! list all potential covariates and distals here}
\StringTok{   female        ! covariate}
\StringTok{   mathjob mathirt;      ! distal math test score in 9th grade "}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 500 100;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File=3step\_savedata.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save=bchweights; ! Here we save the BCH weights}
\StringTok{    format = free;"}\NormalTok{,}

  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat residual tech11 tech14"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{    series = enjoym{-}scaredm(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(lsay\_df),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_df)}

\NormalTok{step1\_fit\_bch }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step1\_bch,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BCH\_Step1.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BCH\_Step1.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Step 2 - Extract BCH Weights}\label{step-2---extract-bch-weights}

Extract saved dataset which is part of the mplusObject ``step1\_fit\_bch''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{output\_bch }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BCH\_step1.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\NormalTok{savedata\_bch }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_bch[[}\StringTok{"savedata"}\NormalTok{]])}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Rename the column in savedata named ``C'' and change to ``N''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_bch)[}\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_bch)}\SpecialCharTok{==}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"N"}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Step 3 - Add Auxiliary Variables and BCH Weights}\label{step-3---add-auxiliary-variables-and-bch-weights}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{step3\_bch  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step3 {-} BCH Method"}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
 \StringTok{"classes = c(4);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  missing are all(9999);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  usevar = BCHW1{-}BCHW4 mathjob mathirt female;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  training = BCHW1{-}BCHW4(bch);"}\NormalTok{ ,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
 \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{  type = mixture; }
\StringTok{  starts = 500 200;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
  \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
 \StringTok{" \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  mathirt on female; ! covariate as a predictor of the distal outcome}
\StringTok{  mathjob on female;}
\StringTok{  C on female;        ! covariate as predictor of C}

\StringTok{  \%C\#1\%}
\StringTok{     }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m1);    ! conditional distal mean }
\StringTok{  mathirt;          ! conditional distal variance (freely estimated)}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j1);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  mathirt on female (rm1);}
\StringTok{  mathjob on female (rj1);}

\StringTok{  \%C\#2\%}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m2);}
\StringTok{  mathirt;}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j2);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  mathirt on female (rm2);}
\StringTok{  mathjob on female (rj2);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  \%C\#3\%}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m3);}
\StringTok{  mathirt;}
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j3);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  mathirt on female (rm3);}
\StringTok{  mathjob on female (rj3);}

\StringTok{  \%C\#4\%}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [mathirt](m4);}
\StringTok{  mathirt; }
\StringTok{  [mathjob](j4);}
\StringTok{  mathjob;}
\StringTok{ }
\StringTok{   mathirt on female (rm4);}
\StringTok{   mathjob on female (rj4);"}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELCONSTRAINT =} 
 
   \StringTok{"New (dm1v2 dm1v3 dm2v3 dm1v4 dm2v4 dm3v4}
\StringTok{   dj1v2 dj1v3 dj2v3 dj1v4 dj2v4 dj3v4}
\StringTok{    );}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{    dm1v2 = m1{-}m2;  ! test pairwise distal mean differences of IRT score}
\StringTok{    dm1v3 = m1{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    dm2v3 = m2{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    dm1v4 = m1{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dm2v4 = m2{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dm3v4 = m3{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    dj1v2 = j1{-}j2;  ! test pairwise distal mean differences of math job}
\StringTok{    dj1v3 = j1{-}j3;}
\StringTok{    dj2v3 = j2{-}j3;}
\StringTok{    dj1v4 = j1{-}j4;}
\StringTok{    dj2v4 = j2{-}j4;}
\StringTok{    dj3v4 = j3{-}j4;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELTEST =} \StringTok{"     ! omnibus test of distal means}
\StringTok{    m1=m2;}
\StringTok{    m2=m3;}
\StringTok{    m3=m4;}
\StringTok{    j1=j2;}
\StringTok{    j2=j3;}
\StringTok{    j3=j4;}
\StringTok{ "}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"Tech1 svalues sampstat"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata\_bch), }
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ savedata\_bch)}

\NormalTok{step3\_fit\_bch }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step3\_bch,}
               \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BCH\_Step3.dat"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"distals"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"three\_step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BCH\_Step3.inp"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The distal means of the outcome variables are located in the `MODEL RESULTS' section. The excerpt below presents the distal means of \emph{math job} and \emph{math IRT score} for Latent Class 1.

\includegraphics[width=4.6875in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_5.png}

Note that the class numbering may not be consistent across different methods. Check the class proportions to make sure that we are comparing the same class across methods. For example, Latent Class 1 in the BCH results matches the Latent Class 4 in the ML 3-step results..

ML-3 step results

\includegraphics[width=4.47917in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_6.png}

BCH approach results

\includegraphics[width=4.47917in,height=\textheight,keepaspectratio]{distals/figures/fig_7.png}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\chapter{Ten Frequently Asked Questions (Nylund-Gibson 7 Choi, 2018)}\label{ten-frequently-asked-questions-nylund-gibson-7-choi-2018}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Example: Positive Youth Development Inventory Analysis}\label{example-positive-youth-development-inventory-analysis}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  The original paper illustrated the modeling ideas described in this article using seven items (see Table 2) from the Positive Youth Development Inventory (PYDI) Contribution subscale (Arnold, Nott, \& Meinhold, 2012) that were administered to 1629 college and university students.
\item
  The PYDI measures behavioral, psychological, and social characteristics that are theorized to indicate positive youth development, and the Contribution Subscale specifically measures the degree to which youth express values and behaviors associated with channeling their positive psychosocial strengths to contribute meaningfully to their local community (Lerner et al., 2005).
\item
  Citation: \href{https://doi.org/10.1037/tps0000176}{Nylund-Gibson, K., \& Choi, A. Y. (2018). Ten frequently asked questions about latent class analysis. \emph{Translational Issues in Psychological Science}, \emph{4}(4), 440--461.}
\end{itemize}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Load packages}\label{load-packages-15}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(tidyverse)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(haven)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(MplusAutomation)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(here)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(janitor)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(gt)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(cowplot)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(DiagrammeR) }
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(webshot2)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(stringr)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(dplyr)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(purrr)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(readr)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(flextable)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(officer)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(glue)}
\FunctionTok{library}\NormalTok{(htmltools)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Variable Description}\label{variable-description-2}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{itemize}
\tightlist
\item
  The original research question was to examine whether this construct of positive contribution comprised qualitatively distinct subtypes among college and university students, and to further examine whether such subtypes (if indeed were present) were meaningfully associated with a demographic predictor and a wellbeing-related outcome.
\end{itemize}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Prepare Data}\label{prepare-data-7}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{df\_qa }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_csv}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lca10faq.csv"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Descriptive Statistics}\label{descriptive-statistics-15}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Set up data to find proportions of binary indicators}
\NormalTok{ds }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ df\_qa }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(PYDI1Ab, PYDI2Ab, PYDI3Ab, PYDI4Ab, PYDI5Ab, PYDI6Ab, PYDI7Ab),}
               \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{) }

\CommentTok{\# Create table of variables and counts, then find proportions and round to 3 decimal places}
\NormalTok{prop\_df }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ ds }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{count}\NormalTok{(variable, value) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{group\_by}\NormalTok{(variable) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prop =}\NormalTok{ n }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(n)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{ungroup}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{prop =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(prop, }\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{))}


\CommentTok{\# Make it a gt() table}
\NormalTok{prop\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ prop\_df }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{groupname\_col =} \StringTok{"variable"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{rowname\_col =} \StringTok{"value"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_stubhead}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*Values*"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"Variable Proportions"}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{variable =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*Variable*"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{value =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*Value*"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{n =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*N*"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{prop =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*Proportion*"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }
  
\NormalTok{prop\_table}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}
