\section{Enumeration}\label{enumeration-6}

This code uses the \texttt{mplusObject} function in the \texttt{MplusAutomation} package and saves all model runs in the \texttt{enum} folder.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lca\_6  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{6}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_enum  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
      
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class"}\NormalTok{), }
  
    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"categorical = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab; }
\StringTok{     MISSING ARE ALL (9999);}
\StringTok{     usevar = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab;}
\StringTok{     classes = c(\{k\}); "}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 500 100; }
\StringTok{    processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat residual tech1 tech11 tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{    series = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(df\_qa),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ df\_qa)}

\NormalTok{lca\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(lca\_enum, }
                     \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lca10faq.dat"}\NormalTok{)),}
                     \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_lca10faq.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
                     \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Examine and Extract Mplus files}\label{examine-and-extract-mplus-files-1}

Check all models for:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\tightlist
\item
  Warnings\\
\item
  Errors\\
\item
  Convergence and Loglikelihood Replication Information
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"extract\_mplus\_info.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Define the directory where all of the .out files are located.}
\NormalTok{output\_dir }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Get all .out files}
\NormalTok{output\_files }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Process all .out files into one dataframe}
\NormalTok{final\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended)}

\CommentTok{\# Extract Sample\_Size from final\_data}
\NormalTok{sample\_size }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{unique}\NormalTok{(final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Sample\_Size)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Table of Fit}\label{table-of-fit-6}

First, extract data

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{output\_lca10faq }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{filefilter =} \StringTok{"lca10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{quiet =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{enum\_extract }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{LatexSummaryTable}\NormalTok{(output\_lca10faq,}
    \AttributeTok{keepCols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Title"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"Parameters"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"LL"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"BLRT\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"T11\_VLMR\_PValue"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"Observations"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{sortBy =} \StringTok{"Title"}\NormalTok{) }

\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ enum\_extract }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CAIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{AWE =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ LL }\SpecialCharTok{+} \DecValTok{2} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ Parameters }\SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{log}\NormalTok{(Observations) }\SpecialCharTok{+} \FloatTok{1.5}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{SIC =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.}\DecValTok{5} \SpecialCharTok{*}\NormalTok{ BIC) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{expSIC =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{max}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{BF =} \FunctionTok{exp}\NormalTok{(SIC }\SpecialCharTok{{-}} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(SIC))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{cmPk =}\NormalTok{ expSIC }\SpecialCharTok{/} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(expSIC)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{9}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{13}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{14}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(Parameters)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Add Convergence percentage, LL Replication percentage, and smallest class (\%) Columns

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Title =} \FunctionTok{str\_trim}\NormalTok{(Title)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    final\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Class\_Model, Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc, }
\NormalTok{             Smallest\_Class, Smallest\_Class\_Perc),}
    \AttributeTok{by =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Title"} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Class\_Model"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Smallest\_Class =} \FunctionTok{coalesce}\NormalTok{(Smallest\_Class, }
\NormalTok{                                   final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class[}\FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(Title, final\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{Class\_Model)])) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{relocate}\NormalTok{(Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc, }\AttributeTok{.after =}\NormalTok{ LL) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Smallest\_Class\_Combined =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(Smallest\_Class, }\StringTok{"\textbackslash{}u00A0("}\NormalTok{, Smallest\_Class\_Perc, }\StringTok{"\%)"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}  
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class, }\SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{Smallest\_Class\_Perc)  }

\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    Title, Parameters, LL,}
\NormalTok{    Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc,  }
\NormalTok{    BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE, }
\NormalTok{    T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BLRT\_PValue, }
\NormalTok{    Smallest\_Class\_Combined,  }
\NormalTok{    BF, cmPk  }
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Then, create table

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{fit\_table1 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Title, Parameters, LL, Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc, }
\NormalTok{         BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE, }
\NormalTok{         T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BLRT\_PValue, }
\NormalTok{         Smallest\_Class\_Combined  }
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Model Fit Summary Table**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_spanner}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"Model Fit Indices"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_spanner}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \StringTok{"LRTs"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BLRT\_PValue)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_spanner}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{label =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Smallest\textbackslash{}u00A0Class"}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(Smallest\_Class\_Combined)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}  
  
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Title =} \StringTok{"Classes"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Parameters =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"npar"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{LL =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*LL*"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{Perc\_Convergence =} \StringTok{"\% Converged"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Replicated\_LL\_Perc =} \StringTok{"\% Replicated"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{BIC =} \StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{aBIC =} \StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{CAIC =} \StringTok{"CAIC"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{AWE =} \StringTok{"AWE"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{T11\_VLMR\_PValue =} \StringTok{"VLMR"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{BLRT\_PValue =} \StringTok{"BLRT"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Smallest\_Class\_Combined =} \StringTok{"n (\%)"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"*Note.* Par = Parameters; *LL* = model log likelihood;}
\StringTok{      BIC = Bayesian information criterion;}
\StringTok{      aBIC = sample size adjusted BIC; CAIC = consistent Akaike information criterion;}
\StringTok{      AWE = approximate weight of evidence criterion;}
\StringTok{      BLRT = bootstrapped likelihood ratio test p{-}value;}
\StringTok{      VLMR = Vuong{-}Lo{-}Mendell{-}Rubin adjusted likelihood ratio test p{-}value;}
\StringTok{      *cmPk* = approximate correct model probability;}
\StringTok{      Smallest K = Number of cases in the smallest class (n (\%));}
\StringTok{      LL Replicated = Whether the best log{-}likelihood was replicated."}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{cells\_title}\NormalTok{()}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{column\_labels.font.weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{6}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{9}\NormalTok{),  }
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue, BLRT\_PValue),  }
    \AttributeTok{fns =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(x), }\StringTok{"—"}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(x }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}} \FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textless{}.001"}\NormalTok{, scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(x, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{01}\NormalTok{)))}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_percent}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(Perc\_Convergence, Replicated\_LL\_Perc),}
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{scale\_values =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}  
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{tab\_style}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{style =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{cell\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{weight =} \StringTok{"bold"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BIC, }\AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ BIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(BIC)),}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ aBIC, }\AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ aBIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(aBIC)),}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ CAIC, }\AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ CAIC }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(CAIC)),}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ AWE, }\AttributeTok{row =}\NormalTok{ AWE }\SpecialCharTok{==} \FunctionTok{min}\NormalTok{(AWE)),}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ T11\_VLMR\_PValue, }
                 \AttributeTok{row =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(T11\_VLMR\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, T11\_VLMR\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{)),}
      \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =}\NormalTok{ BLRT\_PValue, }
                 \AttributeTok{row =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(BLRT\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \FunctionTok{lead}\NormalTok{(BLRT\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, BLRT\_PValue }\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{05}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{    )}
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Print Table

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (knitr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{is\_latex\_output}\NormalTok{()) \{}
  \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{includegraphics[width=}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{textwidth]\{figures/fit\_table1.png\}}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{\} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{  fit\_table1  }
\NormalTok{\}}
\CommentTok{\#\textgreater{} \textbackslash{}includegraphics[width=\textbackslash{}textwidth]\{figures/fit\_table1.png\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Information Criteria Plot}\label{information-criteria-plot-6}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Ensure CAIC exists and is numeric, if it exists}
\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CAIC =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(CAIC))  }\CommentTok{\# Ensure CAIC is numeric}

\CommentTok{\# Ensure CAIC exists and is numeric, if it exists}
\NormalTok{allFit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{CAIC =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(CAIC))  }\CommentTok{\# Ensure CAIC is numeric}

\CommentTok{\# Now, pivot the data}
\NormalTok{allFit }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Title, BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{pivot\_longer}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{cols =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(BIC, aBIC, CAIC, AWE),  }\CommentTok{\# Use these columns for pivoting}
    \AttributeTok{names\_to =} \StringTok{"Index"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{values\_to =} \StringTok{"ic\_value"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Index =} \FunctionTok{factor}\NormalTok{(Index, }\AttributeTok{levels =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"AWE"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"CAIC"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"BIC"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"aBIC"}\NormalTok{))  }\CommentTok{\# Ensure proper ordering of Index}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ Title,}
    \AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ ic\_value,}
    \AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ Index,}
    \AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ Index,}
    \AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ Index,}
    \AttributeTok{lty =}\NormalTok{ Index}
\NormalTok{  )) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =} \FloatTok{2.0}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{size =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_x\_discrete}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_grey}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{end =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{5}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_cowplot}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{"Number of Classes"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Information Criteria Value"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Information Criteria"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{family =} \StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size =} \DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{),  }\CommentTok{\# Change font to Arial}
    \AttributeTok{legend.text =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{family=}\StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{size=}\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.key.width =} \FunctionTok{unit}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"line"}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.title =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
    \AttributeTok{legend.position =} \StringTok{"top"}  
\NormalTok{  )}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{27-lca-faq_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-17-1.pdf}}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Compare Class Solutions}\label{compare-class-solutions-2}

Compare probability plots for \(K = 1:6\) class solutions

\begin{Shaded}

\NormalTok{model\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{data.frame}\NormalTok{()}

\ControlFlowTok{for}\NormalTok{ (i }\ControlFlowTok{in} \DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\FunctionTok{length}\NormalTok{(output\_lca10faq)) \{}
  
\NormalTok{  temp }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ output\_lca10faq[[i]]}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{parameters}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{probability.scale }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}                                       
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model =} \FunctionTok{paste}\NormalTok{(i,}\StringTok{"{-}Class Model"}\NormalTok{))                                                  }
  
\NormalTok{  model\_results }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{rbind}\NormalTok{(model\_results, temp)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\FunctionTok{rm}\NormalTok{(temp)}

\NormalTok{compare\_plot }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}
\NormalTok{  model\_results }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(category }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(est, model, LatentClass, param) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{as.factor}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{str\_to\_lower}\NormalTok{(param))) }

\NormalTok{compare\_plot}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{param }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{fct\_inorder}\NormalTok{(compare\_plot}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{param)}

\FunctionTok{ggplot}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{  compare\_plot,}
  \FunctionTok{aes}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{x =}\NormalTok{ param,}
    \AttributeTok{y =}\NormalTok{ est,}
    \AttributeTok{color =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass,}
    \AttributeTok{shape =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass,}
    \AttributeTok{group =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass,}
    \AttributeTok{lty =}\NormalTok{ LatentClass}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{geom\_point}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+} 
  \FunctionTok{geom\_line}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{scale\_colour\_viridis\_d}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{facet\_wrap}\NormalTok{( }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\NormalTok{ model, }\AttributeTok{ncol =} \DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{labs}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Bullying Items"}\NormalTok{,}
       \AttributeTok{x =} \StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{y =} \StringTok{"Probability"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme\_minimal}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{+}
  \FunctionTok{theme}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{panel.grid.major.y =} \FunctionTok{element\_blank}\NormalTok{(),}
                          \AttributeTok{axis.text.x =} \FunctionTok{element\_text}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{angle =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\DecValTok{45}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hjust =} \SpecialCharTok{{-}}\NormalTok{.}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{))        }
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{27-lca-faq_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-18-1.pdf}}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lca.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lca}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_lca10faq}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{c4\_lca10faq.out)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{27-lca-faq_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-19-1.pdf}}

\emph{Model Selection}:

Fit indices did not converge on a single solution, and this is generally the rule rather than the exception in applied practice. The ICs and the cmP suggested a three-class solution, whereas the likelihood tests supported a four-class solution. Evaluating Figure 2, the most prominent ``elbow'' was at the two-class model, whereas the lowest point for the ICs was at the three-class model. The BF suggested that the three-, four-, and five-class solutions were plausible. Given that the BLRT specifically has been shown to be robust across a diversity of modeling conditions (Nylund, Asparouhov, \& Muthén, 2007), we tentatively selected the four-class solution.

\emph{Model Interpretation}:

The above figure illustrates the conditional item probabilities for the selected 4-class solution, where ``conditional'' again refers to the likelihood of endorsing each item as a function of class membership (e.g., the probabilities are ``conditioned'' on class). The model indicators are labeled on the x axis whereas the y axis presents the metric of the item probabilities (0 to 1). The four classes are defined by the crisscrossing lines, and their preliminary labels are listed at the bottom of Figure 3 with class prevalence (e.g., relative class sizes) in parentheses. We referred to the indicators in Table 2 in evaluating the substantive meaning of the joint patterns of item responses that emerged within each class. The first and largest class is characterized by high response probabilities for all indicators and were thus labeled the Holistic--Collaborative class. Youth in this class are likely to value social contribution overall and pursue pertinent activities in cooperation with others. The second class was labeled Altruistic--Low Selfefficacy and is likely to be comprised of youth who highly value social contribution yet do not believe in their own effectiveness for impactful engagement with their community. The third class was labeled Low Engagement given the characteristically low or ambivalent response probabilities for all model indicators. The fourth class was labeled Holistic--Independent given its similarity to the Holistic--Collaborative class apart from two indicators measuring interpersonal and community engagement. Youth in this class are likely to value social contribution but may prefer pursuing relevant activities in a more independent or introverted manner. Examining Figure 3, it is apparent that these two classes are quite homogenous (and thus not well-separated) in their item responses except on items 1 and 3, which may diminish the model classification statistics.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Including Auxilary Variables}\label{including-auxilary-variables}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 1 - Class Enumeration w/ Auxiliary Specification}\label{step-1---class-enumeration-w-auxiliary-specification-3}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

This step is done after class enumeration (or after you have selected the best latent class model). In this example, the four class model was the best. Now, I am re-estimating the four-class model using \texttt{optseed} for efficiency. The difference here is the \texttt{SAVEDATA} command, where I can save the posterior probabilities and the modal class assignment for steps two and three.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{step1  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step 1 {-} Three{-}Step"}\NormalTok{, }
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
  \StringTok{"categorical = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab; }
\StringTok{   usevar = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab;}
\StringTok{  MISSING ARE ALL (9999);}
\StringTok{   classes = c(4); }
\StringTok{    }
\StringTok{   auxiliary =   ! list all potential covariates and distals here}
\StringTok{   hispanic        ! covariate}
\StringTok{   LifeSatA;      ! distal "}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
   \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{    type = mixture;}
\StringTok{    starts = 0;}
\StringTok{    optseed = 371246;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{SAVEDATA =} 
   \StringTok{"File=3step\_savedata.dat;}
\StringTok{    Save=cprob;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"residual tech11 tech14 svalues(2 4 3 1);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{PLOT =} 
    \StringTok{"type = plot3; }
\StringTok{    series = PYDI1Ab PYDI2Ab PYDI3Ab PYDI4Ab PYDI5Ab PYDI6Ab PYDI7Ab(*);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(df\_qa),}
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ df\_qa)}

\NormalTok{step1\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step1,}
                            \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Step1.dat"}\NormalTok{),}
                            \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"one.inp"}\NormalTok{) ,}
                            \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"plot\_lca.R"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{output\_lsay }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"one.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\FunctionTok{plot\_lca}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{model\_name =}\NormalTok{ output\_lsay)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\pandocbounded{\includegraphics[keepaspectratio]{27-lca-faq_files/figure-latex/unnamed-chunk-21-1.pdf}}

Class 1: Holistic-Collaborative (65\%)
Class 2: Altruistic-Low Self-efficacy (8\%)
Class 3: Low Engagement (5\%)
Class 4: Holistic-Independent (22\%)

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 2 - Determine Measurement Error}\label{step-2---determine-measurement-error-7}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Extract logits for the classification probabilities for the most likely latent class

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{logit\_cprobs }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"class\_counts"}\NormalTok{]]}
\NormalTok{                                       [[}\StringTok{"logitProbs.mostLikely"}\NormalTok{]])}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Extract saved dataset which is part of the mplusObject ``step1\_fit''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{savedata }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(output\_lsay[[}\StringTok{"savedata"}\NormalTok{]])}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Rename the column in savedata named ``C'' and change to ``N''

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata)[}\FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata)}\SpecialCharTok{==}\StringTok{"C"}\NormalTok{] }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \StringTok{"N"}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsection{Step 3 - Add Auxiliary Variables}\label{step-3---add-auxiliary-variables-4}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Model with 1 covariate and 1 distal outcome

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{step3  }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \StringTok{"Step3 {-} 3step LSAY"}\NormalTok{, }
  
  \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} 
 \StringTok{"nominal=N;}
\StringTok{  usevar = n;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  classes = c(4);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  usevar = hispanic LifeSatA;"}\NormalTok{ ,}
  
  \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} 
 \StringTok{"estimator = mlr; }
\StringTok{  type = mixture; }
\StringTok{  starts = 0;"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{DEFINE =} 
   \StringTok{"center hispanic (grandmean);"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODEL =}
  \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
 \StringTok{" \%OVERALL\%}
\StringTok{ }
\StringTok{  LifeSatA on hispanic; ! covariate as a predictor of the distal outcome}
\StringTok{  C on hispanic;        ! covariate as predictor of C}

\StringTok{     \%C\#1\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[1,1]\}]; ! MUST EDIT if you do not have a 4{-}class model. }
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[1,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[1,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [LifeSatA](m1);    ! conditional distal mean }
\StringTok{  LifeSatA;          ! conditional distal variance (freely estimated)}

\StringTok{  \%C\#2\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[2,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[2,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[2,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [LifeSatA](m2);}
\StringTok{  LifeSatA;}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  \%C\#3\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[3,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[3,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[3,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [LifeSatA](m3);}
\StringTok{  LifeSatA;}

\StringTok{  \%C\#4\%}
\StringTok{  [n\#1@\{logit\_cprobs[4,1]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#2@\{logit\_cprobs[4,2]\}];}
\StringTok{  [n\#3@\{logit\_cprobs[4,3]\}];}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{  [LifeSatA](m4);}
\StringTok{  LifeSatA; "}\NormalTok{),}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELCONSTRAINT =} 
   \StringTok{"New (diff12 diff13 diff23 }
\StringTok{    diff14 diff24 diff34);}
\StringTok{  }
\StringTok{    diff12 = m1{-}m2;  ! test pairwise distal mean differences}
\StringTok{    diff13 = m1{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    diff23 = m2{-}m3;}
\StringTok{    diff14 = m1{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    diff24 = m2{-}m4;}
\StringTok{    diff34 = m3{-}m4;"}\NormalTok{,}
  
  \AttributeTok{MODELTEST =} \StringTok{"     ! omnibus test of distal means }
\StringTok{    m1=m2;}
\StringTok{    m2=m3;}
\StringTok{    m3=m4;"}\NormalTok{,}
 
  \AttributeTok{usevariables =} \FunctionTok{colnames}\NormalTok{(savedata), }
  \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ savedata)}

\NormalTok{step3\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(step3,}
               \AttributeTok{dataout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Step3.dat"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{modelout=}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"three\_starts.inp"}\NormalTok{), }
               \AttributeTok{check=}\ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\subsubsection{Wald Test Table}\label{wald-test-table-3}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{modelParams }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{readModels}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"10faq"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"manual\_3step"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"three\_starts.out"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Extract information as data frame}
\NormalTok{wald }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(modelParams[[}\StringTok{"summaries"}\NormalTok{]]) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(WaldChiSq\_Value}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{WaldChiSq\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{WaldChiSq\_DF =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"("}\NormalTok{, WaldChiSq\_DF, }\StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{unite}\NormalTok{(wald\_test, WaldChiSq\_Value, WaldChiSq\_DF, }\AttributeTok{sep =} \StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{pval =}\NormalTok{ WaldChiSq\_PValue) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\textless{}.001*"}\NormalTok{),}
                       \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{                              scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{))))}

\CommentTok{\# Create table}

\NormalTok{wald\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ wald }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Wald Test of Paramter Constraints (Math)"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{wald\_test =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Wald Test (*df*)"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*p*{-}value"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{opt\_align\_table\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  gt}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.font.names =} \StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{wald\_table}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large Wald Test of Paramter Constraints (Math)}
}

\subsubsection{Table of Distal Outcome Differences}\label{table-of-distal-outcome-differences-1}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Extract information as data frame}
\NormalTok{diff }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{as.data.frame}\NormalTok{(modelParams[[}\StringTok{"parameters"}\NormalTok{]][[}\StringTok{"unstandardized"}\NormalTok{]]) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{filter}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{grepl}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"DIFF"}\NormalTok{, param)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  dplyr}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(param}\SpecialCharTok{:}\NormalTok{pval) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{se =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"("}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{format}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(se,}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{nsmall =}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{")"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{unite}\NormalTok{(estimate, est, se, }\AttributeTok{sep =} \StringTok{" "}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{str\_remove}\NormalTok{(param, }\StringTok{"DIFF"}\NormalTok{),}
         \AttributeTok{param =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(param)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{separate}\NormalTok{(param, }\AttributeTok{into =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Group"}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{sep =} \DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{class =} \FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Class "}\NormalTok{, Group1, }\StringTok{" vs "}\NormalTok{, Group2)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(class, estimate, pval) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.001}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\textless{}.001*"}\NormalTok{),}
                       \FunctionTok{ifelse}\NormalTok{(pval}\SpecialCharTok{\textless{}}\FloatTok{0.05}\NormalTok{, }\FunctionTok{paste0}\NormalTok{(scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{), }\StringTok{"*"}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{                              scales}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{number}\NormalTok{(pval, }\AttributeTok{accuracy =}\NormalTok{ .}\DecValTok{001}\NormalTok{))))}

\CommentTok{\# Create table}

\NormalTok{diff }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Distal Outcome Differences"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{class =} \StringTok{"Class"}\NormalTok{,}
      \AttributeTok{estimate =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"Mean (*se*)"}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{pval =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"*p*{-}value"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
    \FunctionTok{sub\_missing}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{,}
              \AttributeTok{missing\_text =} \StringTok{""}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"center"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{opt\_align\_table\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
\NormalTok{  gt}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.font.names =} \StringTok{"serif"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

{\large Distal Outcome Differences}

\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont

\toprule
 & Estimate (\emph{se}) & \emph{p}-value \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
Hispanic & 0.365 (0.72) & 0.614 \\ 
\bottomrule
\end{tabular*}
\end{table}

Following the three-step procedure, the logit values of the classification probabilities for the four-class solution for the first step were copied to be used in the third step. We used these values in a second set of analyses to fix the measurement parameters of the latent classes, and the auxiliary variables relations were estimated thereafter (see Appendix B). We used multinomial logistic regression to evaluate whether the relative proportions of Hispanic and non-Hispanic youth were equal across the four classes, and the results are reported in Table 5. Notably, Hispanic youth (versus nonHispanic) were more likely to be in the Holistic--Collaborative class compared with both the Altruistic--Low Self-efficacy class (OR 2.71, p .001) and the Holistic Independent class (OR 1.62, p .009). No other covariate-class relations were statistically significant. Simultaneously, we estimated classspecific means of life satisfaction for each of the four classes. For the interpretation of distal outcomes, we centered the covariate such that the distal outcome mean differences across the latent classes accounted for the relative proportion of Hispanic versus non-Hispanic youth in the entire sample. Pairwise Wald tests revealed that life satisfaction in the Holistic-Collaborative class was significantly higher compared to each of the other three classes. No other distal mean comparisons were statistically significant.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{References}\label{references}

Arnold, M. E., Nott, B. D., \& Meinhold, J. L. (2012). The Positive Youth Development Inventory full version. Corvallis: Oregon State University.

Lerner, R. M., Lerner, J. V., Almerigi, J. B., Theokas, C., Phelps, E., Gestsdottir, S., . . . von Eye, A. (2005). Positive youth development, participation in community youth development programs, and community contributions of fifth-grade adolescents: Findings from the first wave of the 4-H Study of Positive Youth Development. The Journal of Early Adolescence, 25, 17--71. \url{http://dx.doi} .org/10.1177/0272431604272461

Nylund, K. L., Asparouhov, T., \& Muthén, B. O. (2007). Deciding on the number of classes in latent class analysis and growth mixture modeling: A Monte Carlo simulation study. Structural Equation Modeling, 14, 535--569. \url{http://dx.doi.org/10.1080/} 10705510701575396

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\chapter{The Importance of Early Attitudes Toward Mathematics and Science (Ing \& Nylund-Gibson, 2017)}\label{the-importance-of-early-attitudes-toward-mathematics-and-science-ing-nylund-gibson-2017}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

This document presents a replication of the latent transition analysis (LTA) conducted by Ing and Nylund-Gibson, (2017), which investigated how students' attitudes toward mathematics and science evolve over time and how those attitudinal trajectories relate to later academic outcomes. Using nationally representative longitudinal data from the LSAY study, we follow students from Grade 7 to Grade 12, classifying them into latent attitudinal profiles at each wave and modeling transitions between those profiles across time. This replication reproduces the authors' manual 3-step LTA approach, including: (1) estimating an invariant unconditional model to identify latent profiles across grades; (2) using fixed logits to classify students into profiles at each wave; and (3) examining how class membership and transitions relate to distal outcomes and demographic covariates. Where applicable, we extend the original analysis by visualizing transition patterns, computing subgroup differences using z-tests, and formatting outputs for clear interpretability.

Citation: \href{https://doi.org/10.1177/016146811711900507}{Ing,M., \& Nylund-Gibson, K. (2017). The importance of early attitudes toward mathematics and science. \emph{Teachers College Record: The Voice of Scholarship in Education}, \emph{119}(5), 1-32.}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{read\_sav}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"data"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"Dataset\_Jul9\_Mplus.sav"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Filter to follow{-}up sample (target \textasciitilde{}1824 rows)}

\CommentTok{\# Define survey questions}
\NormalTok{all\_questions }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{"AB39A"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39H"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39I"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39K"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39L"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39M"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39T"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39U"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39W"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"AB39X"}\NormalTok{, }\CommentTok{\# 7th grade}
  \StringTok{"GA32A"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA32H"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA32I"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA32K"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA32L"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA33A"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA33H"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA33I"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA33K"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"GA33L"}\NormalTok{, }\CommentTok{\# 10th grade}
  \StringTok{"KA46A"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA46H"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA46I"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA46K"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA46L"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA47A"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA47H"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA47I"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA47K"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"KA47L"}  \CommentTok{\# 12th grade}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Rename variables \textgreater{}8 characters}
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{rename}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{SCIG8 =}\NormalTok{ ScienceG8,}
    \AttributeTok{SCIG11 =}\NormalTok{ ScienceG11}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Recode 9999 to NA (missing)}
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{across}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{all\_of}\NormalTok{(all\_questions), }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\FunctionTok{if\_else}\NormalTok{(. }\SpecialCharTok{==} \DecValTok{9999}\NormalTok{, }\ConstantTok{NA\_real\_}\NormalTok{, .)))}

\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data) }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{toupper}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{names}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data))}

\section{Descriptive Statistics}\label{descriptive-statistics-16}

\subsection{Create Table 1}\label{create-table-1}

This table provides the overall mean and standard deviation for each attitudinal item at Grades 7, 10, and 12. These values summarize general trends in the full sample and serve as a foundation for the latent class models that follow.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Function to compute stats (count, mean, SD)}
\NormalTok{compute\_stats }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(data, question, grade, question\_name) \{}
\NormalTok{  data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{summarise}\NormalTok{(}
      \AttributeTok{Grade =}\NormalTok{ grade,}
      \AttributeTok{Count =} \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(}\SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(.data[[question]])),}
      \AttributeTok{Mean =} \FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(.data[[question]], }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{),}
      \AttributeTok{SD =} \FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(.data[[question]], }\AttributeTok{na.rm =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{    ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
    \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Question =}\NormalTok{ question\_name)}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Define question names and mappings}
\NormalTok{table\_setup }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \AttributeTok{question\_code =}\NormalTok{ all\_questions,}
  \AttributeTok{grade =} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{7}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{, }\DecValTok{12}\NormalTok{), }\AttributeTok{each =} \DecValTok{10}\NormalTok{),}
  \AttributeTok{question\_name =} \FunctionTok{rep}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"I enjoy math"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Math is useful in everyday problems"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Math helps a person think logically"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"It is important to know math to get a good job"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"I will use math in many ways as an adult"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"I enjoy science"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Science is useful in everyday problems"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"Science helps a person think logically"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"It is important to know science to get a good job"}\NormalTok{,}
      \StringTok{"I will use science in many ways as an adult"}
\NormalTok{    ),}
    \AttributeTok{times =} \DecValTok{3}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Compute stats for all questions}
\NormalTok{table1\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{pmap\_dfr}\NormalTok{(}
  \FunctionTok{list}\NormalTok{(table\_setup}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{question\_code, table\_setup}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{grade, table\_setup}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{question\_name),}
  \SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}}\FunctionTok{compute\_stats}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data, ..}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{, ..}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{, ..}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Mean =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(Mean, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \AttributeTok{SD =} \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(SD, }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{arrange}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{match}\NormalTok{(Question, table\_setup}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{question\_name), Grade) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(Question, Grade, Count, Mean, SD)}

\CommentTok{\# Build table}
\NormalTok{table1\_gt }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ table1\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{groupname\_col =} \StringTok{"Question"}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \StringTok{"Table 1. Descriptive Statistics for Mathematics and Science Attitudinal Survey Items Included In Analyses"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{Grade =} \StringTok{"Grade"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Count =} \StringTok{"N"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{Mean =} \StringTok{"M"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{SD =} \StringTok{"SD"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{fmt\_number}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(Mean, SD),}
    \AttributeTok{decimals =} \DecValTok{2}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Show table}
\NormalTok{table1\_gt}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Create Table 2}\label{create-table-2}

This table presents model fit statistics for latent profile enumeration at Grades 7, 10, and 12. For each grade, models with 1 through 6 profiles were estimated and evaluated using the commonly used information criteria (AIC, BIC, SSA-BIC), entropy, and likelihood ratio tests (LMR, BLRT). These values help determine the most appropriate number of latent profiles to retain at each grade.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# Coerce to numeric just in case}
\NormalTok{lsay\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{MATHG12 =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(MATHG12),}
    \AttributeTok{SCIG11  =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(SCIG11),}
    \AttributeTok{STEMSUP =} \FunctionTok{as.numeric}\NormalTok{(STEMSUP)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\CommentTok{\# Build the summary table}
\NormalTok{table2\_data }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ tibble}\SpecialCharTok{::}\FunctionTok{tibble}\NormalTok{(}
  \StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Outcome Variable}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{"12th Grade Mathematics Achievement"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"11th Grade Science Achievement"}\NormalTok{,}
    \StringTok{"STEM Career Attainmentᵃ"}
\NormalTok{  ),}
  \AttributeTok{N =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12 }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12)),}
    \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11  }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11)),}
    \FunctionTok{sum}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{STEMSUP }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{STEMSUP))}
\NormalTok{  ),}
  \AttributeTok{M =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12[lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12 }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12)]), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11[lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11 }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11)]), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{mean}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{STEMSUP[lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{STEMSUP }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{STEMSUP)]), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ),}
  \AttributeTok{SD =} \FunctionTok{c}\NormalTok{(}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12[lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12 }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{MATHG12)]), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{round}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{sd}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11[lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11 }\SpecialCharTok{!=} \DecValTok{9999} \SpecialCharTok{\&} \SpecialCharTok{!}\FunctionTok{is.na}\NormalTok{(lsay\_data}\SpecialCharTok{$}\NormalTok{SCIG11)]), }\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{),}
    \ConstantTok{NA}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{)}

\CommentTok{\# Render the gt table}
\NormalTok{table2\_gt }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ table2\_data }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Table 2. Descriptive Statistics for Distal Outcome Variables**"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \StringTok{\textasciigrave{}}\AttributeTok{Outcome Variable}\StringTok{\textasciigrave{}} \OtherTok{=} \StringTok{"Outcome Variable"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{N =} \StringTok{"N"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{M =} \StringTok{"M"}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{SD =} \StringTok{"SD"}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{sub\_missing}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{(), }\AttributeTok{missing\_text =} \StringTok{""}\NormalTok{) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_footnote}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{footnote =} \StringTok{"Binary indicator coded 1 = STEM occupation in mid{-}30s follow{-}up."}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{locations =} \FunctionTok{cells\_body}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{rows =} \DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \StringTok{"Outcome Variable"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{table2\_gt}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

  \AttributeTok{filename =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"figures"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"table2\_distal\_outcomes.png"}\NormalTok{)}

\section{Run Independent LCAs for Each Timepoint}\label{run-independent-lcas-for-each-timepoint}

\subsection{Prepare Data for MPlusAutomation}\label{prepare-data-for-mplusautomation}

To determine the appropriate number and structure of attitudinal profiles at each timepoint, we first estimate separate latent class models for Grades 7, 10, and 12. This step involves preparing the data in wide format and running independent LCAs using \texttt{MplusAutomation}. The goal is to identify the optimal number of latent profiles per grade based on model fit before moving to the longitudinal transition model.

\subsection{Run LCA on 7th Grade Items}\label{run-lca-on-7th-grade-items}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{lca\_belonging }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class LCA for LSAY 7th Grade"}\NormalTok{),}

    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"categorical = AB39A AB39H AB39I AB39K AB39L AB39M AB39T AB39U AB39W AB39X;}
\StringTok{       usevar = AB39A AB39H AB39I AB39K AB39L AB39M AB39T AB39U AB39W AB39X;}
\StringTok{       missing = all(9999);}
\StringTok{       classes = c(\{k\});"}
\NormalTok{    ),}

    \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      estimator = mlr;}
\StringTok{      type = mixture;}
\StringTok{      starts = 500 10;}
\StringTok{      processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat; residual; tech11; tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{PLOT =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      type = plot3;}
\StringTok{      series = AB39A AB39H AB39I AB39K AB39L AB39M }
\StringTok{               AB39T AB39U AB39W AB39X(*);"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  lca\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    lca\_enum,}
    \AttributeTok{dataout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g7\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lsay\_g7.dat"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{modelout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g7\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_g7.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{check =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Run LCA on 10th Grade}\label{run-lca-on-10th-grade}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{lca\_belonging }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class LCA for LCA 10th Grade"}\NormalTok{),}

    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"categorical = GA32A GA32H GA32I GA32K GA32L GA33A GA33H GA33I GA33K GA33L;}
\StringTok{       usevar = GA32A GA32H GA32I GA32K GA32L GA33A GA33H GA33I GA33K GA33L;}
\StringTok{       missing = all(9999);}
\StringTok{       classes = c(\{k\});"}
\NormalTok{    ),}

    \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      estimator = mlr;}
\StringTok{      type = mixture;}
\StringTok{      starts = 500 10;}
\StringTok{      processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat; residual; tech11; tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{PLOT =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      type = plot3;}
\StringTok{      series = GA32A GA32H GA32I GA32K GA32L GA33A }
\StringTok{               GA33H GA33I GA33K GA33L (*);"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  lca\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    lca\_enum,}
    \AttributeTok{dataout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g10\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lsay\_g10.dat"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{modelout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g10\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_g10.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{check =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Run LCA for 12th Grade}\label{run-lca-for-12th-grade}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\NormalTok{lca\_belonging }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{lapply}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{1}\SpecialCharTok{:}\DecValTok{8}\NormalTok{, }\ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(k) \{}
\NormalTok{  lca\_enum }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusObject}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{TITLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"\{k\}{-}Class LCA for LCA 12th Grade"}\NormalTok{),}

    \AttributeTok{VARIABLE =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}
      \StringTok{"categorical = KA46A KA46H KA46I KA46K KA46L KA47A KA47H KA47I KA47K KA47L;}
\StringTok{       usevar = KA46A KA46H KA46I KA46K KA46L KA47A KA47H KA47I KA47K KA47L;}
\StringTok{       missing = all(9999);}
\StringTok{       classes = c(\{k\});"}
\NormalTok{    ),}

    \AttributeTok{ANALYSIS =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      estimator = mlr;}
\StringTok{      type = mixture;}
\StringTok{      starts = 500 10;}
\StringTok{      processors = 10;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{OUTPUT =} \StringTok{"sampstat; residual; tech11; tech14;"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{PLOT =} \StringTok{"}
\StringTok{      type = plot3;}
\StringTok{      series = KA46A KA46H KA46I KA46K KA46L KA47A }
\StringTok{               KA47H KA47I KA47K KA47L (*);"}\NormalTok{,}

    \AttributeTok{rdata =}\NormalTok{ lsay\_data}
\NormalTok{  )}

\NormalTok{  lca\_enum\_fit }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{mplusModeler}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    lca\_enum,}
    \AttributeTok{dataout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g12\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"lsay\_g12.dat"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{modelout =} \FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g12\_enum"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"c\{k\}\_g12.inp"}\NormalTok{)),}
    \AttributeTok{check =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{run =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{,}
    \AttributeTok{hashfilename =} \ConstantTok{FALSE}
\NormalTok{  )}
\NormalTok{\})}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Extract Mplus Information

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{\# LCA Extraction}
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"extract\_mplus\_info.R"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{output\_dir\_lca }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"enum"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{output\_files\_lca }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir\_lca, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{final\_data\_lca }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files\_lca, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%} 
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model\_Type =} \StringTok{"LCA"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

\section{Screen Output for Warnings, Errors, and Loglikelihood Replication}\label{screen-output-for-warnings-errors-and-loglikelihood-replication}

After estimating each LCA model, we examine the Mplus output files for warnings, estimation errors, and loglikelihood replication issues. This quality check helps ensure that solutions are trustworthy and that selected models are not based on local maxima or convergence failures. In this step, we flag any estimation concerns and verify that the best loglikelihood value is replicated consistently across random starts.

\begin{center}\rule{0.5\linewidth}{0.5pt}\end{center}

Extract Warnings from Output Files

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"extract\_mplus\_info.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# Extract 7th grade LCAs}
\NormalTok{output\_dir\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g7\_enum"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{output\_files\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir\_g7, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{final\_data\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files\_g7, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model\_Type =} \StringTok{"LCA"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{Grade =} \StringTok{"7th"}\NormalTok{)}


\CommentTok{\# Extract 10th grade LCAs}
\NormalTok{output\_dir\_g10 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g10\_enum"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{output\_files\_g10 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir\_g10, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{final\_data\_g10 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files\_g10, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model\_Type =} \StringTok{"LCA"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{Grade =} \StringTok{"10th"}\NormalTok{)}


\CommentTok{\# Extract 12th grade LCAs}
\NormalTok{output\_dir\_g12 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"g12\_enum"}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{output\_files\_g12 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{list.files}\NormalTok{(output\_dir\_g12, }\AttributeTok{pattern =} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{.out$"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{full.names =} \ConstantTok{TRUE}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{final\_data\_g12 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{map\_dfr}\NormalTok{(output\_files\_g12, extract\_mplus\_info\_extended) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{mutate}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{Model\_Type =} \StringTok{"LCA"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{Grade =} \StringTok{"12th"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Examine Output Warnings}\label{examine-output-warnings}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"extract\_warnings.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-} 7th Grade {-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{warnings\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{extract\_warnings}\NormalTok{(final\_data\_g7) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{left\_join}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{select}\NormalTok{(final\_data\_g7, File\_Name), }\AttributeTok{by =} \StringTok{"File\_Name"}\NormalTok{)}

\NormalTok{warnings\_table\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}}\NormalTok{ warnings\_g7 }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Model Warnings — 7th Grade LCA**"}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
    \AttributeTok{File\_Name =} \StringTok{"Output File"}\NormalTok{,}

\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
  \FunctionTok{cols\_width}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{    File\_Name }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{150}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{    Warning\_Summary }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{150}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{    Warnings }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{400}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{  ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}

Save Warning Tables

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{gtsave}\NormalTok{(warnings\_table\_g7, }\AttributeTok{filename =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"figures"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"warnings\_g7\_lca.png"}\NormalTok{))}
\FunctionTok{gtsave}\NormalTok{(warnings\_table\_g10, }\AttributeTok{filename =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"figures"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"warnings\_g10\_lca.png"}\NormalTok{))}
\FunctionTok{gtsave}\NormalTok{(warnings\_table\_g12, }\AttributeTok{filename =} \FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"figures"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"warnings\_g12\_lca.png"}\NormalTok{))}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Extract Errors from Output Files

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\FunctionTok{source}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{here}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"tc\_lta"}\NormalTok{,}\StringTok{"functions"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"error\_visualization.R"}\NormalTok{))}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-} 7th Grade Errors {-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{error\_table\_g7 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{process\_error\_data}\NormalTok{(final\_data\_g7)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-} 10th Grade Errors {-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{error\_table\_g10 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{process\_error\_data}\NormalTok{(final\_data\_g10)}

\CommentTok{\# {-}{-}{-}{-} 12th Grade Errors {-}{-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{error\_table\_g12 }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \FunctionTok{process\_error\_data}\NormalTok{(final\_data\_g12)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Examine Errors}\label{examine-errors}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]

\CommentTok{\# Helper to conditionally render or notify}
\NormalTok{render\_error\_table }\OtherTok{\textless{}{-}} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(error\_df, grade\_label) \{}
  \ControlFlowTok{if}\NormalTok{ (}\FunctionTok{nrow}\NormalTok{(error\_df) }\SpecialCharTok{\textgreater{}} \DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{) \{}
\NormalTok{    error\_df }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{gt}\NormalTok{() }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{tab\_header}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{title =} \FunctionTok{md}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"**Model Estimation Errors — \{grade\_label\} Grade**"}\NormalTok{))) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{cols\_label}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{File\_Name     =} \StringTok{"Output File"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{Class\_Model   =} \StringTok{"Model Type"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{Error\_Message =} \StringTok{"Error Message"}
\NormalTok{      ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{cols\_align}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{align =} \StringTok{"left"}\NormalTok{, }\AttributeTok{columns =} \FunctionTok{everything}\NormalTok{()) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{cols\_width}\NormalTok{(}
\NormalTok{        File\_Name     }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{150}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{        Class\_Model   }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{100}\NormalTok{),}
\NormalTok{        Error\_Message }\SpecialCharTok{\textasciitilde{}} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{400}\NormalTok{)}
\NormalTok{      ) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{tab\_options}\NormalTok{(}\AttributeTok{table.width =} \FunctionTok{px}\NormalTok{(}\DecValTok{600}\NormalTok{)) }\SpecialCharTok{\%\textgreater{}\%}
      \FunctionTok{fmt}\NormalTok{(}
        \AttributeTok{columns =} \StringTok{"Error\_Message"}\NormalTok{,}
        \AttributeTok{fns =} \ControlFlowTok{function}\NormalTok{(x) }\FunctionTok{gsub}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{, }\StringTok{"\textless{}br\textgreater{}"}\NormalTok{, x)}
\NormalTok{      )}
\NormalTok{  \} }\ControlFlowTok{else}\NormalTok{ \{}
    \FunctionTok{cat}\NormalTok{(}\FunctionTok{glue}\NormalTok{(}\StringTok{"✅ No errors detected for \{grade\_label\} Grade.}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\NormalTok{))}
\NormalTok{  \}}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{\# Print or notify for each grade}
\FunctionTok{render\_error\_table}\NormalTok{(error\_table\_g7, }\StringTok{"7th"}\NormalTok{)}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{table}[t]
\caption*{
{\large \textbf{Model Estimation Errors --- 7th Grade}}
} 
\fontsize{12.0pt}{14.4pt}\selectfont
\begin{tabular*}{450pt}{@{\extracolsep{\fill}}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{\dimexpr 112.50pt -2\tabcolsep-1.5\arrayrulewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{\dimexpr 75.00pt -2\tabcolsep-1.5\arrayrulewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{\dimexpr 300.00pt -2\tabcolsep-1.5\arrayrulewidth}}
\toprule
Output File & Model Type & Error Message \\ 
\midrule\addlinespace[2.5pt]
c2\_g7.out & 2-Class & RANDOM STARTS TO CHECK THAT THE BEST LOGLIKELIHOOD IS STILL OBTAINED AND REPLICATED. \\ 
c3\_g7.out & 3-Class & RANDOM STARTS TO CHECK THAT THE BEST LOGLIKELIHOOD IS STILL OBTAINED AND REPLICATED. \\ 
c4\_g7.out & 4-Class & RANDOM STARTS TO CHECK THAT THE BEST LOGLIKELIHOOD IS STILL OBTAINED AND REPLICATED. IN THE OPTIMIZATION, ONE OR MORE LOGIT THRESHOLDS APPROACHED EXTREME VALUES OF -15.000 AND 15.000 AND WERE FIXED TO STABILIZE MODEL ESTIMATION.  THESE VALUES IMPLY PROBABILITIES OF 0 AND 1.  IN THE MODEL RESULTS SECTION, THESE PARAMETERS HAVE 0 STANDARD ERRORS AND 999 IN THE Z-SCORE AND P-VALUE COLUMNS. \\
